Article Title: 'Don't have time to watch videos': CJI Surya Kant declines intervention in Jantar Mantar protest crackdown
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Supreme Court on Wednesday declined a request for an urgent hearing of a petition challenging the alleged "police brutality" against students at Jantar Mantar saying, "we do not have time to watch videos. "The matter was mentioned before Chief Justice of India Surya Kant-led bench, also comprising Justices Joymalya Bagchi and V Mohana.Seeking an urgent listing, the petitioner's counsel told the court that students had allegedly been subjected to police brutality during Monday's protest and said the plea contained three prayers."I have the videos as well regarding police brutality... If it can be listed tomorrow (Thursday)... students are there," the lawyer submitted.The CJI, however, declined the request saying, "We are not interested in videos, we don't have time to watch... We don't want to watch videos."When the counsel again referred to the video evidence and alleged that students had been beaten up, the CJI responded, "Don't waste our time. We don't want to watch any videos," according to news agency PTI.The petition relates to the police action during the CJP's 'Sansad Chalo' march on Monday, the opening day of Parliament's Monsoon Session.Thousands of students and young protesters had marched towards Parliament demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over alleged irregularities in competitive examinations.The protest turned violent after demonstrators attempted to breach police barricades. Security personnel used lathis and tear gas to disperse the crowd, with both protesters and police reporting injuries.Meanwhile, the CJP's sit-in at Jantar Mantar entered its second month on Wednesday, with the organisation claiming that more supporters joined the protest overnight.Volunteers at the protest site continued receiving food and other supplies sent by supporters, even as heavy security remained deployed in the area following Monday's clashes.The agitation began on June 20, with protesters seeking accountability over alleged examination irregularities, reforms in the education system and the resignation of education minister Dharmendra Pradhan.Activist Sonam Wangchuk, who joined the protest on June 28, continues his indefinite hunger strike from Medanta Hospital after being shifted there from Safdarjung Hospital following a Delhi high court order.Wangchuk has said he will continue his fast until accountability is fixed in the education system or political leaders assure him that the issue will be taken up in Parliament.The developments come months after CJI Surya Kant while hearing a separate case likened some unemployed youngsters to "cockroaches", saying they end up becoming media persons, social media users and RTI activists."There are youngsters like cockroaches, who don't get any employment and don't have any place in the profession. Some of them become media, some of them become social media, some of them become RTI activists, some of them become other activists, and they start attacking everyone," he had said.The remarks were made in an unrelated case in which a lawyer had challenged the Delhi high court's process for designating senior advocates. The court eventually allowed the petitioner to withdraw the plea.
Article Summary: Supreme Court declines a request for an urgent hearing of a petition challenging the alleged "police brutality" against students at Jantar Mantar. The petition relates to the police action during the CJP's 'Sansad Chalo' march on Monday. Thousands of students and young protesters had marched towards Parliament demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan.

Article Title: 'Does India run on your arrogance?' BJP tears into Rahul Gandhi over PM residence march
Article Text:
BJP calls Rahul's march to PM residence 'irresponsible conduct'

NEW DELHI: A day after Rahul Gandhi's march to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence ended in a confrontation with police, the BJP mounted a sharp political counteroffensive on Wednesday, accusing Lok Sabha leader of opposition of disregarding the dignity of the Prime Minister's residence and announcing nationwide protests against Congress party.The saffron party also called out Rahul Gandhi's "Chalo PM Residence" as "irresponsible" and "highly condemnable." Leading the attack, senior party leader Ravi Shankar Prasad asked Lok Sabha LoP: "'Does this country run on your arrogance?"Addressing a presser in New Delhi, senior BJP leader and former Union minister Ravi Shankar Prasad took a swipe at Rahul Gandhi's political legacy, saying that someone from a family that produced three Prime Ministers should have understood the dignity attached to the Prime Minister's residence."He is sitting on a dharna at the gate of the Prime Minister's residence. His father Rajiv Gandhi was the Prime Minister, his grandmother Indira Gandhi was the Prime Minister, and his great-grandfather Jawaharlal Nehru was also the Prime Minister of India. He, of all people, should know the dignity and sanctity of the Prime Minister's residence. Despite this, the kind of irresponsible behaviour he has displayed is highly condemnable," Prasad said.Announcing the BJP's response, Prasad said the saffron party would stage protests across the country outside Congress offices."The BJP will hold protests today across the country against Congress outside Congress offices for the unruly protest outside the PM's house yesterday," he said.Questioning Rahul Gandhi's approach, Prasad accused the Congress leader of abandoning dialogue and trying to dictate terms to the government."How do you intend to function? Will you just sit there forcibly? You were engaged in a dialogue. They were ready for a debate on the NEET issue, but then you backed out. Does this country run on your stick? Will it run on your arrogance? Rahul Gandhi, you have been irresponsible. I condemn your entire conduct," he said.Defending the police action during the protest, the BJP leader said security personnel had no option but to intervene to maintain law and order in the high-security zone."Later, the police were forced to take action, which was necessary for security and for maintaining peace. The Prime Minister has always been clear on these issues," Prasad said.Shifting focus to the controversy surrounding competitive examinations, Prasad asserted that the government had acted swiftly to protect the integrity of recruitment and entrance tests."In the past, irregularities were reported in several states during various examinations. Consequently, foolproof exams were conducted immediately after cancellations. The results have been declared with a 56-57% success rate, and students are satisfied. These exams were held without charging any additional fees," he told reporters.Emphasising the Centre's commitment to students, Prasad said those responsible for exam irregularities had been arrested and were facing trial."All the candidates appearing for NEET and other exams are our children. It is our duty to be concerned about their future, and we will fulfill that responsibility. Those involved in irregularities have been arrested and are facing trial. The government is ensuring that their trial leads to certain punishment," he said.The senior BJP leader concluded by reiterating Prime Minister Narendra Modi's assurance that the sanctity of NEET and other examinations would be protected through systemic reforms and stricter scrutiny.Meanwhile, another voice from the ruling BJP joined the attack on Rahul Gandhi. Senior BJP leader and MP Giriraj Singh told reporters, "Rahul Gandhi has tarnished the dignity of the Constitution while holding a constitutional position. His conduct is extremely dangerous. A case of sedition should be filed against Rahul Gandhi."The BJP's attack came a day after Rahul led the Opposition's march towards Lok Kalyan Marg, the official residence of the Prime Minister, where protesters came face-to-face with security personnel in the high-security Lutyens' Delhi zone.Videos from the scene showed police moving in to disperse the protesters gathered outside the Prime Minister's residence. In one video shared by the Congress on its official X handle, Rahul Gandhi was seen lying on the road as police personnel attempted to lift and remove him from the protest site. The former Congress president appeared to resist being taken away as security officials surrounded him.The Congress also released a photograph showing Rahul nosebleed following the police action, alleging excessive use of force during the protest.Rahul Gandhi's protest came amid Jantar Mantar protest demanding Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation over accountability in the NEET paper-leak.
Article Summary: Rahul Gandhi's march to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence ended in a confrontation with police. The saffron party called out Rahul Gandhi's "Chalo PM Residence" as "irresponsible" and "highly condemnable" Senior party leader Ravi Shankar Prasad asked Rahul: "'Does this country run on your arrogance?"

Article Title: NEET protest: TVK slams govt; Rahul, Priyanka released from detention — top developments
Article Text:
The developments came as thousands of protesters returned to Jantar Mantar to continue the agitation.

FIR filed as video shows RAF jawan assaulted

TVK condemns Rahul's detention, seeks NEET abolition



Rahul, Priyanka released after detention



Pradhan hits out at Congress



Police detail injuries, register six FIRs



Sonam Wangchuk continues fast



NEW DELHI: Congress leaders Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra were released after being detained by Delhi Police during a protest near Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence. The party had marched to Lok Kalyan Marg demanding the resignation of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the alleged paper leak and the police action against student protesters.The protests also drew support from several opposition leaders, while Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) strongly criticised the Centre over Rahul Gandhi's detention and renewed its demand for the abolition of NEET. Meanwhile, the BJP accused the Congress of politicising the issue and defended the police action, saying the protest near the Prime Minister's residence was held without permission.The developments came as thousands of protesters returned to Jantar Mantar to continue the agitation.Delhi Police registered an FIR after a Rapid Action Force (RAF) jawan was allegedly chased and assaulted by a group of protesters during the Cockroach Janta Party's (CJP) "Sansad Chalo" march in New Delhi.A video of the incident, widely shared on social media, appears to show the jawan being surrounded, pursued and beaten during Monday's protest. Police said an investigation is under way to identify those responsible.Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) condemned the detention of Rahul Gandhi and criticised the police action against opposition leaders."The crushing of democracy in the capital is strongly condemnable! The arrest of Lok Sabha Opposition Leader Mr. Rahul Gandhi, who joined the field in support of the youth's non-violent protest held in Delhi, by being dragged by the Delhi Police and carried away in handcuffs, is a blatant attack on democratic rights. This is strongly condemnable," the party said.TVK also renewed its opposition to the NEET examination."From the moment the NEET exam was implemented, not only has its credibility been lacking, but students continue to face mental stress, with their future dreams turning into a massive question mark. The harm caused to students every year due to the NEET exam shows no sign of stopping. Therefore, we strongly demand that the Union government completely abolish the NEET exam," it said.Rahul Gandhi, Priyanka Gandhi Vadra and several opposition leaders were detained after Congress workers marched towards the Prime Minister's residence at Lok Kalyan Marg.Rahul Gandhi, Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav and other leaders were taken to Chhatrasal Stadium before being released later in the day. Priyanka Gandhi was taken to Mandir Marg Police Station, where Congress Parliamentary Party chairperson Sonia Gandhi later arrived.Congress leaders said they protested because they were not allowed to raise the NEET issue in Parliament. Rahul Gandhi said they marched to the Prime Minister's residence "to demand answers from him for the brutalities against young students yesterday" and demanded the resignation of the Prime Minister and the Home Minister.The protest was joined by Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge, KC Venugopal, Pawan Khera, Karnataka chief minister DK Shivakumar, Kerala Leader of Opposition VD Satheesan, Supriya Sule and several other opposition leaders.Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan accused the Congress of using students for political purposes."LoP Shri @RahulGandhi and @INCIndia continue to shamelessly exploit students as political tools to manufacture disruption during the Monsoon Session of Parliament," he wrote on X.He said the government was ready for a discussion on NEET in Parliament but alleged that the Congress preferred "political spectacle over democratic debate"."We owe our students more than outrage. We owe them answers, reforms and accountability. That is exactly what our government remains committed to delivering," Pradhan said.BJP MP Sambit Patra also criticised the protest, saying demonstrations should be held only at designated locations. He said the Prime Minister's residence is a high-security zone and staging a protest there without permission was "inappropriate".Delhi Police said six FIRs have been registered in connection with the violence during Monday's Parliament march. Two FIRs were registered at Parliament Street police station, while one each was registered at Connaught Place, Tilak Marg, Barakhamba Road and Kartavya Path police stations.DCP Northeast Delhi Rahul Alawal said six police personnel were injured during the violence. He said Constable Mohit suffered serious injuries to his head, below his eyes and on his legs and remains on medical rest. Additional DCP Sandeep Lamba, Inspector Rampal Goswami and some other officers have resumed duty despite being advised to rest.Station House Officer of Connaught Place police station Sukhbir Singh Malik said he was assaulted when a section of protesters allegedly turned violent near Regal Cinema. Malik said his helmet prevented a head injury, but he suffered injuries to his elbows, calf muscle and waist after being dragged to the ground. The police said several other personnel, including an ACP, an Inspector, a Head Constable and a driver, were also injured.Activist Sonam Wangchuk continued his indefinite hunger strike after being shifted from Safdarjung Hospital to Medanta Hospital in Gurugram following directions from the Delhi High Court.Sources told news agency ANI said Union Health Minister JP Nadda and Minister of State in the Prime Minister's Office Jitendra Singh visited Medanta Hospital to enquire about Wangchuk's health.The Delhi High Court directed Medanta Hospital to form a specialised medical team to supervise Wangchuk's treatment while allowing him to continue his fast under constant medical supervision. His wife, Gitanjali Angmo, said he would continue the hunger strike in solidarity with students allegedly injured during the police action.
Article Summary: Congress leaders Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra were released after being detained by Delhi Police during a protest. The party had marched to Lok Kalyan Marg demanding the resignation of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) strongly criticised the Centre over Rahul Gandhi's detention and renewed its demand for the abolition of NEET.

Article Title: 'Government had no interest in debate': Rahul Gandhi on why Congress protested outside PM's residence
Article Text:
Rahul issued a video message explaining why they staged a protest, demands debate on paper leak in Parliament

NEW DELHI: The leader of opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi issued statement addressing the students and Prime Minister Narendra Modi after the Congress party's protest outside PM residence over the NEET paper leak.He shared a video message on X, saying it was his "direct message to the students of India and the Prime Minister." He backed the students and demanded a proper discussion in Lok Sabha on Wednesday during the ongoing Monsoon Session of the Parliament"This morning I met the Speaker of the Lok Sabha and told him that it was important that we be allowed to debate what happened to the students. The previous day, the Speaker had said he needed to take permission from the government. But it became very clear to us in the hours that followed that the government had no interest in holding a debate," he said.Continuing his allegations, he added, "So, we took a decision, that we will stage a protest in front of Prime Minister's house and get the students' issues to national attention"Further questioning the govt, he added, "Why is the Indian education system collapsing? Why are papers leaking? Why is education so expensive in India? Why do families have to financially ruin themselves to educate their children? These are legitimate questions, and there is absolutely nothing wrong with asking them."Rahul also alleged that cases were being filed against students and asserted that the entire Opposition stood with them. Reiterating the demands he said, "The students' demands are, firstly, the resignation of Dharmendra Pradhan and HM Amit Shah. Secondly, action should be taken against those who have beaten and humiliated the students, and all the cases against the students should be withdrawn. Finally, the entire opposition wants that there should be a debate on this tomorrow in the Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha on this issue. .."Further addressing PM Modi he said, "The entire Opposition stands with the students. We want reforms in the examination and education systems. We will continue to fight for your voice and your future."This comes after Rahul along with Priyanka Gandh iwere detained for protesting outside PM residence. They were later released. The Congress leaders tied plastic bands around their hands and raised slogans, demanding the resignation of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the NEET paper leak.Read more: How Congress surprised its own MPs, govt with protest outside PM's residence
Article Summary: Rahul Gandhi issues a video message to students and Prime Minister Narendra Modi. He demands debate on paper leak in Parliament during Monsoon Session. He also alleged that cases were being filed against students. This comes after Rahul along with Priyanka Gandh iwere detained for protesting outside PM residence.

Article Title: Omar Abdullah ‘flees’ Jantar Mantar in auto, says CJP supporters followed him
Article Text:
Omar Abdullah travelled in the auto-rickshaw along with his two sons, Zahir Abdullah and Zamir Abdullah. Photo credit: X @OmarAbdullah

SRINAGAR: Jammu & Kashmir CM Omar Abdullah Tuesday said his now-viral autorickshaw ride after Monday’s protest in New Delhi for restoration of statehood was a sudden decision prompted by concerns after supporters of the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) started following him.Videos of Omar sitting inside a green-and-yellow autorickshaw, carrying protest placards after the demonstration, have spread all across social media. “It was completely spontaneous,” he said, talking to reporters after a presser.“I started walking back after the protest when some of my party workers joined me. Then the Cockroach Party people also began following us,” Omar said. “I noticed the Delhi Police was getting very jumpy, with more police vehicles arriving. I thought these Cockroach Party supporters might end up being tear-gassed as people would assume I was leading them towards Rajpath and Parliament. I didn’t want that on my head,” he said. “Then I saw an autorickshaw, jumped in and left.”Earlier, addressing a press conference, Omar justified the protest for statehood in New Delhi but excluded restoration of Article 370 from it, saying the incumbent govt at the Centre, which has revoked the special status of J&K, will not restore it.“I have always told the people of Jammu & Kashmir that expecting the same people who took Article 370 away to hand it back to us on a platter is unrealistic,” he said. “We have to work to restore J&K’s special status, but we are not going to get it under this govt.”Omar said restoration of statehood is the first step towards any discussion on restoring special status for J&K, arguing that special status has little meaning without a state’s legislative powers. “What does Article 370 mean without a state? What is the meaning of Article 371 without a state?” he said.Asked about the absence of senior Congress leaders like Rahul Gandhi during the protest, Omar said the circumstances in New Delhi made it difficult for many opposition leaders to participate. “Those of us who were affected by the tear gas know what the situation was like.”He said postponing the protest was not a practical option despite the disruption.About his US president Donald Trump remarks on J&K statehood, he said, “I have never internationalized Kashmir. Did I ask Mr Trump to get our statehood?”
Article Summary: Jammu & Kashmir CM Omar Abdullah said his now-viral autorickshaw ride after Monday’s protest in New Delhi for restoration of statehood was a sudden decision prompted by concerns. “I noticed the Delhi Police was getting very jumpy, with more police vehicles arriving. I thought these Cockroach Party supporters might end up being tear-gassed as people would assume I was leading them towards Rajpath and Parliament,” he said.

Article Title: 'Ghor paap': PM Modi on NEET paper leak; vows strict action against culprits
Article Text:
PM Modi, according to Rijiju, also said that "security and future of youth" is the government's priority.

The Monday mayhem

NEW DELHI: Prime Minister Narendra Modi termed the NEET paper leak issue "ghor paap (grave sin)" and assured strict action against the culprits who "play with the future of youth". The reaction came during the NDA's parliamentary party meeting and was conveyed by Union minister Kiren Rijiju Rijiju, who is the minister of parliamentary affairs, said the prime minister stated that "NEET paper leak should not be an issue of partisan politics" as it is a "national issue and not a matter of one state or the Centre alone".PM Modi, according to Rijiju, also said that "security and future of youth" is the government's priority.The Prime Minister's reaction comes in the backdrop of protest led by youth and Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) members, who were protesting against the perpetual paper leaks, and alleged irregularities in the education system.The uproar over the NEET leak and lack of accountability at the senior-most level exploded in the Capital on Monday as protestors, mostly students and youths, clashed with police -- leading the cops to resort to lathi charge, firing of tear gas to disperse the protesting crowd marching towards the Parliament.The protesters had three main demands: Resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan, discharging Sonam Wangchuk from hospital and providing compensation to the kin of NEET aspirants who died by suicide after the leak forced the government to re-examination.More than 170 people, including Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protesters and Delhi Police personnel, were treated at central government hospitals on Monday following clashes during the 'Chalo Sansad' march, with a young woman admitted to Safdarjung Hospital in critical condition, sources said.According to hospital sources, around 150 injured people were taken to Dr Ram Manohar Lohia (RML) Hospital, while at least 24 others received treatment at Lady Hardinge Medical College (LHMC). Many of those brought to both hospitals were discharged after receiving first aid.Officials at RML said that by 7 pm, the hospital's surgery department had registered 65 medico-legal cases (MLCs) involving both protesters and police personnel. They added that detailed information on all cases was still being compiled.Hospital authorities also said around 10 MLCs involved people who had been on hunger strike. All of them were reported to be in stable condition.Sources at RML said nearly 40 of the injured brought to the hospital were police personnel, while the remaining patients were protesters. Around 35 people were admitted, with the rest treated and discharged. Between seven and eight patients required treatment in the hospital's red zone because of the seriousness of their injuries.Among those admitted was a 20-year-old woman who suffered a hypoxic brain injury, a condition caused when the brain is deprived of adequate oxygen following severe trauma, hospital sources said.The exact circumstances in which she sustained her injuries remain unclear. According to hospital sources, she may have been injured during the clashes or after falling in the stampede that followed, but the sequence of events has not yet been established.Doctors said most of the remaining patients suffered bruises, baton injuries, head wounds and facial injuries, including split lips, forehead wounds and scalp lacerations. Although many had visible injuries to the head and face, no fractures were reported among patients treated at RML.At Lady Hardinge Medical College, at least 24 injured people were treated. Most were discharged after first aid, while those who arrived later in the evening continued to undergo medical evaluation. Doctors at the hospital said none of the patients brought there had sustained major injuries.
Article Summary: PM Narendra Modi termed the NEET paper leak issue "ghor paap (grave sin)" and assured strict action against the culprits. The reaction came during the NDA's parliamentary party meeting. PM Modi, according to Rijiju, also said that "security and future of youth" is the government's priority.

Article Title: No legal aid to accused, SC orders fresh trial in 30-year-old blast case
Article Text:
SC set aside conviction and death sentence of an accused in Rajasthan’s 1996 Samleti bus blast case

NEW DELHI: Holding that legitimacy of a judicial verdict is measured not by severity of the punishment but by the fairness of the processes through which guilt is determined, Supreme Court Tuesday set aside the conviction and death sentence of an accused in Rajasthan's 1996 Samleti bus blast case in which 14 people had died, noting that meaningful legal assistance was not made available to him to defend himself.To find out if the accused, Abdul Hameed, was represented by a lawyer, SC interacted with him through video from jail and analysed the order sheet of the trial court. It concluded that the accused remained undefended in court proceedings.A bench of Justices Vikram Nath, Sanjay Karol and Sandeep Mehta directed a fresh trial be conducted in the three-decade-old case."The right to effective legal representation, the presumption of innocence, the burden upon the prosecution to establish guilt beyond reasonable doubt and the requirement that evidence be scrutinised with objectivity and caution are not mere procedural formalities. They are substantive guarantees that distinguish the rule of law from the rule of public opinion," the court observed."Justice must not only be done but must manifestly be seen to be done. The legitimacy of a judicial verdict rests not merely upon the outcome reached, but equally upon the fairness, transparency and integrity of the process by which that outcome is achieved," it added.The court admitted that acts of violence directed against innocent civilians strike at the very foundations of social order and generate understandable feelings of fear, grief and outrage but said it was precisely in such cases that courts must remain steadfast in their adherence to constitutional values."The gravity of the allegation cannot be permitted to dilute the standard of proof, nor can the enormity of the crime justify a departure from settled principles governing criminal adjudication. The greater the public outcry, the greater becomes the responsibility of the court to ensure that the determination of guilt is based solely upon law and evidence," it said.The court requested acting Chief Justice of Rajasthan HC to designate a special court in Jaipur to conduct the de novo trial (retrial). It said the special court shall be presided over by an officer of the Rajasthan higher judicial service having not less than seven years' experience of conducting sessions trials and it shall make every endeavour to conclude the trial within a year from the date of assignment of the case.It said if the accused is unable to engage a counsel, the special court shall secure competent legal representation for him to ensure effective and meaningful legal assistance throughout the proceedings.
Article Summary: Supreme Court Tuesday set aside the conviction and death sentence of an accused in Rajasthan's 1996 Samleti bus blast case. A bench of Justices Vikram Nath, Sanjay Karol and Sandeep Mehta directed a fresh trial be conducted in the three- decade-old case.

Article Title: CJP removes spokesperson Vijeta Dahiya, condemns 'deeply insensitive actions'
Article Text:
Cockroach Janta Party removes spokesperson Vijeta Dahiya

Controversial viral video

NEW DELHI: The Cockroach Janta Party has removed its spokesperson Vijeta Dahiya after viral videos surfaced showing him during the police crackdown on protesters at Jantar Mantar, calling his conduct "deeply insensitive" and "unacceptable. ""We strongly condemn the deeply insensitive actions of our spokesperson Vijeta Dahiya, whose videos surfaced while peaceful protestors and our team were facing brutal police violence. Such conduct is unacceptable, shows a complete lack of judgment, and is wholly inconsistent with the values and principles of our movement," the party said in a statement.Dahiya has been removed as spokesperson and relieved of all official duties, the party said.The clip, which went viral on social media, showed Dahiya at a burger outlet in a mall near the protest site. A group of people can be heard confronting him and questioning why he wasn't with the protesters, asking: "You were eating a burger while we were protesting?"The exact timing of the video and the circumstances in which it was recorded could not be independently verified.In an Instagram video addressing the backlash, Dahiya said he had been working continuously for the movement for around one and a half months and had barely slept for the past two nights.He questioned why eating a burger had become such a major issue. In the caption, he also made a comment stating that: “By the way, I didnt eat the burger, because I felt that it has maida and transfats, and maybe I should eat elsewhere.”"No one pays me to participate in the protest," he said, adding that he was doing what he was doing for the country. "Yes, I will eat the burger," he said.Before this incident Dahiya had also come under fire on social media for his views on transgender people and stating that most women are 'not political'.The move comes as the CJP continues its protest at Jantar Mantar demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over alleged examination irregularities. Police had earlier resorted to a baton charge and detained several protesters during a march to Parliament on Monday.
Article Summary: Cockroach Janta Party has removed its spokesperson Vijeta Dahiya after viral videos surfaced showing him during the police crackdown on protesters at Jantar Mantar. The clip, which went viral on social media, showed Dahiya at a burger outlet in a mall near the protest site. Dahiya has been removed as spokesperson and relieved of all official duties.

Article Title: 'Went where they least expected us': Priyanka Gandhi on 'Chalo PM House' protest
Article Text:
'Marched to PM Modi's house to demand answers'

'Join our dharna'

Union minister Jitendra Singh meets Rahul

Jitendra Singh claims Rahul changed demands mid-negotiation

Police remove Rahul from protest site

'Modi Ji, try every pressure'

Sonia reaches police station after Rahul, Priyanka detained

'Went where they least expected us'

'Wah! kya jugalbandi hai'

Opposition leaders joins protest

Rahul, Priyanka meet admitted students

Pradhan slams

Congress protest

outside PM residence

NEW DELHI: Senior Congress leaders, including Rahul Gandhi , Mallikarjun Kharge and Priyanka Gandhi, on Tuesday took their protest to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence at Lok Kalyan Marg, alleging police excesses against students during the Cockroach Janata Party's march to Parliament.Congress MPs and senior leaders raised slogans against PM Narendra Modi and the Bharatiya Janata Party over the NEET paper leak and demanded the resignation of PM Narendra Modi and education minister Dharmendra Pradhan.In a social media post on X, Rahul said that he marched to PM Modi's residence to demand answers for the brutalities against students on Monday."We have marched to PM Modi’s house to demand answers from him for the brutalities against young students yesterday. The government doesn’t want to take any accountability or does it want to have a debate on it in Parliament," Rahul said."PM and HM must resign for destroying the future of India’s youth," he added.In another social media post on X, Rahul called on "patriotic Indians" to join the protest outside the PM's residence."An attack on students is an attack on every Indian family. PM Modi believes he can get away without answers, without consequences.He cannot. Not this time," Rahul said."I ask every patriotic Indian who believes our students deserve justice - join us in dharna in front of the Prime Minister’s residence. The voice of India’s students will not be ignored," he added.Nearly 20 minutes into the protest, minister of state in the Prime Minister's Office Jitendra Singh met Rahul at the protest site and urged him to end the dharna.After holding talks with Rahul, Jitendra Singh accused the LoP of backtracking from his earlier demands and refusing to end the dharna.In a social media post on X, Singh said, "Some time ago, it came to light that the Congress Party's national president, Shri Mallikarjun Kharge, and other senior leaders with him, such as Shri Rahul Gandhi, Smt. Priyanka Gandhi, and others, suddenly sat down for a sit-in protest near Akbar Road with their supporters. In view of the dignity of this senior delegation, the government sent me personally, along with the Union home secretary, Shri Govind Mohan, to the spot to hold talks with these senior leaders. A request was made to Shri Rahul Gandhi to end his sit-in, as this site is not meant for protests, and holding a sit-in here is causing considerable inconvenience to the general public."Also Read| 'Rahul wanted a confrontation': Jitendra Singh details fallout of talks with Congress leader "Shri Rahul Gandhi put forward the demand that if the government agreed to discuss in Parliament all issues related to NEET and the associated movement, he would immediately end his sit-in. Within a few moments, after taking permission from the government's top leadership, Shri Rahul Gandhi was assured that his demand had been accepted, and the government was ready to discuss in Parliament all issues related to NEET and the associated movement," he added.Singh further said that after the government's message was conveyed to Rahul, he also sought assurance on the resignation of education minister Dharmendra Pradhan."When this information was conveyed to Shri Rahul Gandhi, he said that he would not agree to this alone but also sought assurance on the resignation of the Union education minister. He said that now I have two demands: discussion in Parliament and the resignation of the Union education minister. When he was reminded that earlier he had only demanded discussion, he said that now my demands have changed. When an attempt was made to humbly explain to him that for a senior leader like him to backtrack on his word so quickly does not befit him, he said that it is his prerogative. When the Home secretary tried to explain that this site is not suitable for a sit-in, his response was that it is also his prerogative to sit wherever he wants," the Union minister said."For a senior leader like Rahul Gandhi and the leader of the opposition to go back on his word is unfortunate and contrary to every norm of democracy. The government is ready to discuss in Parliament all issues related to NEET and the associated movement. In contrast, the Congress Party has not given any notice to date for a formal discussion on this serious matter as per the rules. Shri Rahul Gandhi's behavior does not align with the principles of democracy," he added.Meanwhile Delhi Police tried to forcibly remove Rahul, Priyanka and Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav from the protest site.While being detained by the Delhi Police, Congress MP Priyanka Gandhi Vadra said, "They are cowards. They are scared. We are not scared of them. They are scared of us."Additionally, police also detained several senior Congress leaders, including Charanjit Singh Channi, MP K Suresh, and Kanhaiya Kumar."This is BJP's terrorism. They don't listen to the students," Channi said as he was being taken away by the Delhi Police.Congress MP D K Suresh said that the police were "brutally detaining Congress workers.""Brutally, they are detaining us. We are protesting in front of the Prime Minister's residence because our students were brutally attacked yesterday by the Delhi Police. The Prime Minister is not saying anything, the home minister is not making any statement," he said.After he was detained, Rahul again took to X and said that fight against students will not stopped."Modi Ji, try every pressure, exert all your might - this fight for justice for students will now not stop or be halted."After detaining Rahul and Priyanka from the protest site, the Delhi Police took the leaders to Chhatrasal Stadium Police Station and Mandir Marg Police Station."Leader of opposition Rahul Gandhi has been detained at Chhatrasal Stadium. And General Secretary Priyanka Gandhi ji has been brought to Mandir Marg Police Station. If this is the cost to be paid for fighting for the youth -- so be it," Congress leader Supriya Shrinate said in a post on X.Later, veteran Congress leader Sonia Gandhi arrived at Mandir Marg Police Station. Following her arrival, a huge crowd gathered outside the police station.Released after the detention, Priyanka said that students should be heard over alleged irregularities and a discussion should be held in Parliament."If the leader of the opposition wishes to speak in Parliament, he is not allowed to do so. If we seek permission to stage a protest, that permission is denied. So we went to a place where they least expected us," Priyanka said."What is the problem in listening to the children of our country, the future of our nation? They have a genuine demand. As their representatives, it is our responsibility to raise and empower their voices. In a democracy, protesting is a fundamental right. We will raise our voice wherever it is required. Our demand is clear and remains the same from the beginning. We have to fix the broken education system. Dharmendra Pradhan has to resign. A discussion should be held in Parliament. And the students should be heard," she added.Meanwhile, former Delhi chief minister Atishi took a jibe at Congress' protest and said that CJP and Sonam Wangchuk were protesting at Jantar Mantar for the past one month but the government refused to talk to them, but initiated dialogue with Rahul Gandhi within one hour of his protest."This is amazing! CJP and Sonam Wangchuk: One month protest at Jantar Mantar. Modi govt refuses to talk to them," Atishi said in a social media post on X."Rahul Gandhi: Allowed to protest outside PM residence. In one hour Modi Govt starts dialogue. Wah! Kya jugalbandi hai!" she added.Meanwhile, opposition leaders, including Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav and NCP (SP) leader Supriya Sule, also joined the protest in support of the Congress' demand for accountability over the alleged police action against students.Several MPs from INDIA bloc parties were also seen at the protest site, with the opposition presenting a united front as it sought to mount pressure on the government over the handling of the students' agitation and examination irregularities.Earlier in the day, Rahul and Priyanka met students admitted to the Ram Manohar Lohia (RML) Hospital after they were injured in police action during the CJP-led protest.They also met the mother of an injured young woman protester who is on ventilator support at the RML Hospital, news agency PTI reported, citing sources.In a post on X, the Congress said Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi, along with General Secretary (Organisation) K C Venugopal, met the students injured during the "police brutality and lathi charge" in Monday's youth protest."Today, the future of students in the country is being trampled, and lathis are being rained down on those who raise their voices, for which Narendra Modi should apologise. The students' demand is absolutely clear -- the government should take responsibility for the paper leak and Dharmendra Pradhan should resign," the party said.This comes a day after violent clashes erupted in Delhi as thousands of protesters from the Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) and students marched from Jantar Mantar towards Parliament, demanding the resignation of Dharmendra Pradhan over recurring examination irregularities, including the NEET-UG paper leaks.Pradhan, in his statement, accused Rahul Gandhi and Congress of "shamelessly exploiting students as political tools" following the grand-old-party's protest outside PM Modi's residence over the NEET paper leak.In a social media post on X, Pradhan said Congress chose to stage a dharna outside PM Modi's residence, causing inconvenience to the public and disregarding established security protocols."LoP Shri Rahul Gandhi and Congress continue to shamelessly exploit students as political tools to manufacture disruption during the Monsoon Session of Parliament," the education minister said.Read more: Education minister Pradhan slams Cong protest
Article Summary: Senior Congress leaders, including Rahul Gandhi, Mallikarjun Kharge and Priyanka Gandhi, on Tuesday took their protest to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence at Lok Kalyan Marg. Congress MPs and senior leaders raised slogans against PM Narendra Modi and the Bharatiya Janata Party over the NEET paper leak. Rahul said that he marched to PM Modi's house to demand answers for the brutalities against students on Monday.

Article Title: Pilgrimages to shrines in J&K stay suspended for 3rd day amid hostile weather
Article Text:
Pilgrims have been advised to stay at the base camps and lodging centres

JAMMU: Pilgrimages to the cave shrines of Shri Amarnath, Mata Vaishnodevi, Shiv Khori, and Sarthal Devi in Jammu & Kashmir remained suspended for the third consecutive day on Tuesday amid heavy rainfall and hostile weather conditions. Schools have been closed till July 22, officials said.Pilgrims have been advised to stay at the base camps and lodging centres in view of the inclement weather, they said.Meanwhile, heavy rains lashed parts of Udhampur district on Tuesday, triggering mudslides at multiple locations along the Jammu-Srinagar National Highway (NH-44), forcing the authorities to divert traffic as a precaution.In Rajouri, the administration on Tuesday issued flood advisory for vulnerable areas and urged precautionary evacuation.Police in Poonch, in coordination with the NDRF, continued search and rescue operations for the third consecutive day in the rain-affected areas of Surankote, the worst affected area in Jammu region due to downpour.Meanwhile, Leader of opposition (LoP) in J&K legislative assembly, Sunil Sharma, Tuesday asked CM Omar Abdullah to suspend political activities and focus on relief and rehabilitation for the people hit by natural calamities in Poonch, Rajouri and other areas of the union territory.“We urge the govt to stand with the people in their hour of crisis and provide them with required relief,” Sharma said at a presser at BJP office in Jammu, in an apparent reference to National Conference’s scheduled protest in Delhi on July 20 to press its demand for the restoration of statehood for Jammu & Kashmir.
Article Summary: Pilgrimages to the cave shrines of Shri Amarnath, Mata Vaishnodevi, Shiv Khori, and Sarthal Devi in Jammu & Kashmir remained suspended for the third consecutive day on Tuesday. Schools have been closed till July 22.

Article Title: Opposition using protest to find political relevance: BJP
Article Text:
A day after the Chalo Sansad march

NEW DELHI: BJP had a go at opposition parties Tuesday as Aam Aadmi Party attacked Congress over its dharna outside PM's residence on the NEET-UG paper leak issue, saying it is quite obvious that the "sponsored" protests were never about students but about the opposition's search for political relevance."It is quite obvious that the sponsored protests were never about students. No affected student was given a platform. Opposition politicians frequented Jantar Mantar in search of political relevance, and now, we see Congress and AAP squabbling over credit," BJP said as the Arvind Kejriwal-led party accused Congress of trying to weaken Cockroach Janta Party's protest.Keeping up its attack over the protests, BJP alleged that all political assets of those resenting India's rise have been activated. "But India's democracy is neither so fragile nor are the people of this country so naive that they cannot see their mandate being held to ransom by the sponsored outrage of a few," BJP's Amit Malviya said, adding the timing is no coincidence.As India accelerates towards becoming a developed nation, every transformational initiative is met with a familiar cycle of disruption, disinformation and manufactured outrage, he claimed.Malviya said at a time when the country is making rapid strides in different sectors, those who failed to stop India's rise electorally now seek to slow it through perpetual agitation, manufactured crises and a relentless campaign to erode public confidence.
Article Summary: Aam Aadmi Party attacked Congress over its dharna outside PM's residence on the NEET-UG paper leak issue. BJP said it is quite obvious that the "sponsored" protests were never about students. BJP alleged that all political assets of those resenting India's rise have been activated.

Article Title: 4 out of 10 healthcare workers worldwide face violence: WHO
Article Text:
The incident took place at the Shastri Nagar Hospital, run by the Kalyan Dombivli Municipal Corporation (KDMC), in Kalyan on Monday evening.

MUMBAI: As the medical fraternity continues to press for stronger legal protection following Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre's assault on doctors at Dombivli's Shastri Nagar Municipal Hospital, Indian Medical Association (IMA) Tuesday shared an interactive digital grid documenting 100 assaults on doctors reported from across the country in recent years.The campaign comes even as studies show that violence against healthcare workers by patients or their relatives has emerged as a global public health concern. A review published in the indexed medical journal, Atencion Primaria, found that doctors in both developed and developing countries face workplace violence. It reported lifetime prevalence rates of 91% in Germany, 90% in China, 84.5% in Peru, 84% in Turkey and 77.3% in India.According to WHO, four in 10 healthcare workers experience physical violence during their careers. When verbal abuse is included, the proportion rises to over 60%.The triggers for the attacks are universal, be it Mumbai or Singapore: overcrowded hospitals, staff shortages, long waiting times, poor communication and unrealistic patient expectations.Dr Santosh Kadam, former state IMA chief blames the attacks on impatience."We are becoming an impatient society. Everyone wants speedy results. People are spending money, so they want a 100% result," he said.Experts caution that ha-rsher laws alone are unlikely to solve the problem. They underline the need for a combination of measures, including greater investment in healthcare, stronger enforcement of laws, improved doctor-patient communication and awareness campaigns. Many Euro-pean countries observe Ma-rch 12 as a day to raise awareness about violence against healthcare professionals.In India, 19 states have enacted laws to protect healthcare workers, attacks continue. "What we need is one national law,'' said Dr Suhas Pingle, former Maharashtra Medical Council member.In Maharashtra, 738 cases were registered under Maharashtra Medicare Service Persons and Medicare Service Institutions (Prevention of Violence and Damage or Loss to Property) Act between 2010 and 2021, but only four ended in convictions.Spain strengthened legal protection by recognising assaults on healthcare workers as crimes against the state; assaults dropped by half. In Singapore, those who abuse healthcare workers can face a fine of up to S$5,000, jail for up to 12 months, or both.In India, Dr Kadam said, abuse is far more widespread than official records indicate. Dr Pingle said, "Medical practice has become tech-driven and expectations of patients are sky high. Barely 2% of the budget is meant for healthcare. Doctors have to see hundreds of patients in a short span of time. This creates an environment where the risk of violence is high," he said.
Article Summary: The Indian Medical Association (IMA) Tuesday shared an interactive digital grid documenting 100 assaults on doctors reported from across the country in recent years. The campaign comes even as studies show that violence against healthcare workers by patients or their relatives has emerged as a global public health concern.

Article Title: SC seeks Maharashtra zila parishads poll schedule by Friday
Article Text:
The bench asked the SEC to come out with an election schedule by Friday.

NEW DELHI: Supreme Court Tuesday sought Maharashtra State Election Commission’s response by Friday on issuance of a notification to conduct polls in Aug-Sept for remaining 20 zila parishads and 211 nagar panchayats and brushed aside SEC’s ongoing SIR exercise as the reason for the delay in holding polls.Senior advocate Balbir Singh, appearing for SEC, told a bench of CJI Surya Kant, and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and Vipul M Pancholi that most of state govt officers, who are likely to be engaged in the election work related to zila parishads and nagar panchayats, are at present engaged in carrying out the SIR of poll rolls in the state.When the SEC said it intended to conduct these elections in Feb-March next year after the completion of SIR work, the bench said SIR is conducted by EC and not SEC. “We will ensure you get adequate manpower,” it said.The bench asked the SEC to come out with an election schedule by Friday.The batch of matters before the bench raised myriad yet sensitive issues. One challenged the transposition of socially and economically backward classes (SEBC), identified for reservation in govt employment and admission to govt schools and colleges, with reserving constituencies in elections. It said OBCs are politically advanced and powerful in most of the states and a five-judge SC bench in its 2010 judgement in Krishna Murthy case had mandated that constituencies should be reserved for politically backward sections.
Article Summary: The bench asked the SEC to come out with an election schedule by Friday. The batch of matters before the bench raised myriad yet sensitive issues. One challenged the transposition of socially and economically backward classes (SEBC) with reserving constituencies in elections.

Article Title: Won't spare paper leakers, making exams foolproof: PM
Article Text:
PM Modi emphasised 'safety, security and future of the youth', said Rijiju

'Dhurandhar lawyers must ensure punishment for paper-leak accused'

Poll Vote & Share your view What do you think should be the main priority in addressing paper leaks? Stricter action against offenders Improving technology to secure exams Better training for exam officials 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

Also read:

NEW DELHI: PM Modi Tuesday said govt is taking measures to make the exam system "foolproof" and called for shunning partisan politics over paper leaks that have surfaced in several states and under different dispensations, sending out a message of firm intent to end the menace amid attempts by the opposition to seize the issue to corner the Centre."Safety, security and future of the youth is our priority", parliamentary affairs minister Kiren Rijiju quoted PM as telling the meeting of the NDA parliamentary party. PM's address at the meet - a regular affair during Parliament sessions - was focused on youth and paper leaks."Govt will spare no means to ensure stringent punishment to the accused behind NEET paper leak. Those responsible will not be spared," he quoted Modi as saying.PM Modi also emphasised that gangs involved in paper leaks have been active in many states — in what was seen as a reference to leaks happening over a period of time in states — as the Prime Minister highlighted the arrests of NEET-UG paper leak accused, the medical entrance test being held again and declaration of results without delay.The PM also referred to enactment of the Public Examinations (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 2024, by his govt — the first central law of its kind — to deal with the menace of paper leaks.PM Modi said states and Centre should join hands to ensure not only strict punishment to the guilty, but also to rule out such an incident (NEET-UG paper leak) in future, parliamentary affairs minister Kiren Rijiju, quoting from the PM’s speech made at the closed-door meeting.“Dhurandhar” lawyers should see to it that the accused are punished and do not go scot-free, he said, according to some MPs present in the meeting, in what some saw as a reference to the presence of several well-known advocates in opposition’s ranks who regularly appear for anti-govt activists.Modi dwelt at length on the contribution of the youth in leading India’s growth in different sectors.“PM spoke about the country’s youth and that they should get correct guidance. He also shared experiences of the youth who have made the country proud not just nationally but also internationally,” BJP ally and Union minister Chirag Paswan said.The PM asked MPs to inform people about the achievements of Indian startups, noting that French President Emmanuel Macron was impressed with their work when he was in the country recently.He repeated his jibe at the leader of the opposition in the Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi, who is 56, while referring to the average age, 28, of people at Skyroot Aerospace behind the recent successful launch of a rocket. “If they have done so well at 28, imagine how much they will achieve when they reach 56,” the PM said.
Article Summary: PM Modi emphasised'safety, security and future of the youth', said Rijiju. 'Dhurandhar lawyers must ensure punishment for paper-leak accused', he said. PM also referred to enactment of the Public Examinations (Prevention of Unfair Means) Act, 2024.

Article Title: India summons Russian envoy, protests hit that killed 4 Indians on ship
Article Text:
Four Indian nationals were killed after a commercial vessel, MV Golden Leo, came under attack

NEW DELHI: India summoned Russian charge d'affaires, Vladimir Ladanov, to protest the attack on a commercial vessel in Ukraine that left four Indian seafarers dead. The ministry of external affairs (MEA) said in a statement that it conveyed India's grave concerns and unequivocal condemnation of the attack on commercial vessel MV Golden Leo, emphasising that such attacks undermine the safety, security, and stability of international maritime commerce.The Russian diplomat was requested to convey India's concerns to his authorities that the targeting of commercial shipping and the resulting loss of civilian lives are unacceptable and must be avoided, according to the ministry.The Guinea-Bissau-flagged vessel, carrying crew members from Syria and India, was hit by Russian cruise missiles while leaving the port of Odesa in Ukraine. This is the first time that Indian seafarers have been killed in the Russia-Ukraine conflict.Kremlin spokesperson, Dmitry Peskov, told reporters that Moscow was in touch with India on the matter. "We are explaining our position. Most importantly, our armed forces are striking - and will continue to strike - vessels involved in transporting ammunition, weapons, and so on for the purposes of the Kyiv regime," he said.The vessel had 17 crew members, including five Indian nationals, according to the MEA.
Article Summary: India summoned Russian charge d'affaires, Vladimir Ladanov, to protest the attack on a commercial vessel in Ukraine. The Guinea-Bissau-flagged vessel, carrying crew members from Syria and India, was hit by Russian cruise missiles while leaving the port of Odesa. This is the first time that Indian seafarers have been killed in the Russia-Ukraine conflict.

Article Title: CJP's 'Sansad Chalo': 2 protestors injured; 21-year-old on ventilator, another had pellet in eye
Article Text:
2 protestors injured

Nadda meets injured at hospitals

NEW DELHI: Two protesters injured on Monday remained hospitalised on Tuesday, with the rest being discharged. While a 21-year-old woman on ventilator support at RML Hospital is showing signs of improvement, doctors said she continues to be in critical condition, and it is too early to make any prognosis.Another protester, Sheikh Irshad, underwent surgery at Lady Hardinge Medical College to remove a pellet lodged in his left eye. Doctors said another injured protester, identified as Sahil, who is suspected to have sustained a serious eye injury, reached LHMC and was later referred to AIIMS for specialised treatment.Union health minister J P Nadda visited both hospitals during the day to assess the condition of the injured.Doctors at RML Hospital said the 21-year-old woman, identified as Sakshi, continued to require ventilator support but had shown signs of clinical improvement since admission.Hospital officials said all other injured protesters brought to RML had been discharged after treatment. Most had suffered minor injuries, including bruises, cuts to the forehead and lips, and chest injuries.At Lady Hardinge Medical College, doctors said Sheikh Irshad, believed to be around 25 years old, underwent successful surgery on Tuesday to remove a pellet that had entered his left eye. He was stable after the procedure but may remain under observation for another two to three days. Irshad's case came into focus after Cockroach Janta Party chief spokesperson Saurav Das shared a video on X showing Irshad in hospital.
Article Summary: Two protesters injured on Monday remained hospitalised on Tuesday, with the rest being discharged. A 21-year-old woman on ventilator support at RML Hospital is showing signs of improvement. Another protester, Sheikh Irshad, underwent surgery at Lady Hardinge Medical College to remove a pellet lodged in his left eye.

Article Title: J&K anti-drug drive: 2,581 drug traffickers held, 1,431kg narcotics seized in 100 days
Article Text:
In the past 100 days, J&K authorities arrested 2,581 drug traffickers

SRINAGAR: In the past 100 days, J&K authorities arrested 2,581 drug traffickers, seized 1,431 kg of narcotics, including 23.3 kg of heroin, and confiscated assets worth over Rs 188.8 crore under the “Drug-Free J&K” campaign to cripple narco-terror financing, Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha said Tuesday.“We are determined to neutralise the drug cartels and narco-terror networks that target our youth. By revoking hundreds of driving licences, vehicle registrations, recommending suspension of hundreds of passports, and by acting against non-compliant pharmacies, the administration has sent a clear message that no offender is above the law,” Sinha said at an event organised by the social welfare department in Srinagar.The first 100 days of the campaign laid the foundation for the rigorous path ahead, Sinha said, adding: “The road will be tough, but our resolve to wipe out narco-terrorism is stronger than ever.”Sinha said he travelled throughout the Union Territory in the past 100 days, connecting with communities in all 20 J&K districts through foot marches. “Standing alongside families in their darkest moments allowed me to grasp the depth of their anguish.Yet amid that pain, I saw an unwavering hope and a shared conviction that our youth can break free from addiction and reclaim their lives,” he said. “During my march, I saw both pain and courage. I witnessed despair but also glimpses of hope ... such unprecedented public support for this campaign has never been seen in the history of J&K,” he added.The LG urged people to speak up against drug abuse, adding that govt needs the support of the entire community. He advised youth against sacrificing their dreams to drugs. “Never let a drug dealer dictate the course of your life. Your energy is meant for building, your talent for creation, and your courage for moving forward. Take pride in your culture, your heritage, and who you are, and step confidently into a brighter future,” he said.“I appeal to parents to never ignore any signs of drug abuse in their children. We must remember that seeking help in time is wisdom. History bears witness that whether it was terrorism or narco-terror, silence has caused the greatest harm to society. So, break the silence and spread awareness so we can save millions of lives,” Sinha said.Sinha said the administration will soon launch the “Rehabilitation and Socio-Economic Reintegration Scheme for Drug Abuse Victims, 2026,” starting with a pilot in two sensitive districts of J&K. The initiative, built on a structured three-year cycle, guides individuals through medical care and therapy in Phase 1, education and employment in Phase 2, and long-term relapse prevention with community support in Phase 3.
Article Summary: In the past 100 days, J&K authorities arrested 2,581 drug traffickers, seized 1,431 kg of narcotics, including 23.3 kg of heroin. Lieutenant Governor Manoj Sinha: “We are determined to neutralise the drug cartels and narco-terror networks that target our youth”

Article Title: IED found in J&K’s Rajouri, destroyed
Article Text:
IED found in Rajouri

JAMMU: Alert Army personnel discovered an Improvised Explosive Device (IED) during a search in Dalhori area of J&K’s Rajouri district late Monday.Following the recovery, the Bomb Disposal Squad was alerted. A team rushed to the site in Argi village and destroyed the IED in a controlled explosion around 10pm, officials said.Officials said an investigation was underway.
Article Summary: Army personnel discovered an Improvised Explosive Device (IED) during a search in Dalhori area of J&K’s Rajouri district. Bomb Disposal Squad was alerted. A team rushed to the site in Argi village and destroyed the IED in a controlled explosion around 10pm.

Article Title: Israeli man held with ‘narcotics’ in Ladakh’s Zanskar
Article Text:
Police arrested an Israeli national

JAMMU/ZANSKAR: Police arrested an Israeli national after seizing suspected narcotics from his possession during a routine checking drive at Kargyak Check Post in Zanskar, Ladakh, officials said Tuesday.Police identified the suspect as Adir Yemini (24). He was moving in a private vehicle from Manali towards Zanskar when he was caught. The recovery included 104g of suspected charas, 16g of suspected ganja, and a negligible quantity of a heroin-like substance, an official said.An FIR was registered against the accused under the NDPS Act at Zanskar PS. The seized items would be sent for forensic examination. Further investigation was underway, police said.
Article Summary: Police identified the suspect as Adir Yemini (24) He was moving in a private vehicle from Manali towards Zanskar when he was caught. The recovery included 104g of suspected charas, 16g of suspect ganja, and a negligible quantity of a heroin-like substance.

Article Title: Pakistan intruder detained along international border in J&K’s Akhnoor
Article Text:
The suspect claimed he set out from Looni village in Pakistan (Representative image)

JAMMU: BSF detained a 53-year-old Pakistani man who allegedly crossed over the international border in J&K’s Akhnoor sector and sneaked into Indian territory late Monday.“BSF personnel deployed at Malabela border outpost in Khour area spotted suspicious movement and warned the intruder to stop. The suspect, however, continued to proceed, prompting troops to detain him,” an official said.“The suspect, who was taken to Malabela BOP for questioning, identified himself as Mohammad Rafiq alias Kala from Pindi village in Pakistan. A Pakistani ID card, spectacles, cigarette packs, a lighter, tobacco, some eatables, a nail cutter and wallet were recovered during frisking,” said the official.The suspect claimed he set out from Looni village in Pakistan to meet his married sister in Jung village, also in Pakistan, but had accidentally crossed into Indian territory after losing his way. “Investigations are underway to ascertain the circumstances surrounding the border-crossing. The suspect’s claims are being verified,” said an official source.
Article Summary: BSF detained a 53-year-old Pakistani man who allegedly crossed over the international border in J&K’s Akhnoor sector. The suspect claimed he set out from Looni village in Pakistan to meet his married sister in Jung village, also in Pakistan, but had accidentally crossed into Indian territory.

Article Title: The sudden dharna: How Congress surprised its own MPs, govt with protest outside PM's residence
Article Text:
How Congress chalked out the protest plan

NEW DELHI: Chaos erupted in the heart of New Delhi after several Congress leaders, including Rahul Gandhi Mallikarjun Kharge and Priyanka Gandhi , marched to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence at Lok Kalyan Marg and staged a protest against the alleged brutality committed against students during the Cockroach Janata Party's march to Parliament.During the protest, Congress leaders tied plastic bands around their hands and raised slogans, demanding the resignation of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and education minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the NEET paper leak.However, the protest came as much of a surprise to many Congress leaders as it did to the government.According to news agency PTI, the Congress leadership did not tell its leaders about the protest to maintain secrecy, as otherwise the area could have been blockaded.MPs were told that they needed to reach party president Mallikarjun Kharge's residence -- 10 Rajaji Marg -- to wish him on his birthday, PTI reported, citing party sources.Therefore, many MPs were oblivious to what was coming and reached Kharge's residence. They were then asked to follow Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra as the top leaders suddenly moved towards the gate of 7, Lok Kalyan Marg, which is about 500 metres from Kharge's house.Other party leaders also had no clue about the development, and they reached the spot as soon as word spread.Only the top leaders were aware of the move, and they reached Kharge's residence after both Houses of Parliament were adjourned. Most MPs, however, had no clue about the plan.The surprise sit-in by the Congress top brass in the high-security zone sent the government into a tizzy, prompting it to rush minister of state in the PMO Jitendra Singh, along with Union home secretary Govind Mohan, to persuade the leaders of the opposition to vacate the sensitive site.After meeting Rahul, Jitendra Singh accused him of shifting his demands during the talks.Speaking to news agency ANI, Singh said he and Union home secretary Govind Mohan were deputed by the government to meet senior Congress leaders after they began a sit-in protest at a location that, according to him, was not designated for demonstrations and was causing inconvenience to the public."It was learned that Congress Party national president Mallikarjun Kharge and other senior leaders like Rahul Gandhi, Priyanka Gandhi and others had suddenly started a protest. It was also reported that the place was not very conducive to a sit-in protest. It was causing significant inconvenience to the public," he said.The minister said the government sent the two officials to persuade the Congress leaders to shift the discussion to Parliament instead of continuing the protest.Later, Rahul , Priyanka , along with Samajwadi Party leader Akhilesh Yadav, were forcibly removed by the police and taken to police stations at Mandir Marg and Chhatrasal Stadium.
Article Summary: Congress leaders staged a protest against alleged brutality committed against students during Cockroach Janata Party's march to Parliament. The protest came as much of a surprise to many Congress leaders as it did to the government. Most MPs, however, had no clue about the plan and reached the spot as soon as word spread.

Article Title: 'Monsoon Session will be washed out': Congress warns Centre over NEET leak, police action on youth protesters
Article Text:
Parliament session will be washed out: Jairam Ramesh demands Amit Shah's statement on police brutality against student protestors

NEW DELHI: The Congress on Tuesday warned that the ongoing Monsoon Session of Parliament could be a "washout" if the government continued to refuse discussions on the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak and the police action against youth protesters who attempted to march to Parliament a day earlier.Congress MP Jairam Ramesh's remarks came after Delhi Police detained opposition leaders who joined a surprise protest led by the Congress outside Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence.He added that Union home minister Amit Shah, under whose ministry the Delhi Police functions, must make a statement on the police action against protesters.""Our demand has been, and will continue to be, that the home minister makes a statement and that there is a detailed discussion regarding the education system, the examination system, and the atrocities committed against students by the Delhi Police yesterday. Furthermore, we demand the resignation of the education minister (Dharmendra Pradhan)... However, I do not see that happening. If the government maintains this mindset, then the Parliament session will definitely be washed out," the Rajya Sabha MP told ANI.Ramesh further stated that the united stand by opposition parties reflected growing concern over the handling of the examination controversy and the alleged police excesses against students."Opposition unity and solidarity were displayed in a very powerful and impactful manner," he added.Earlier, Leader of Opposition in the Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi, Congress MP Priyanka Gandhi Vadra and several other Congress leaders were detained by Delhi Police during a protest near Prime Minister Narendra Modi's official residence at Lok Kalyan Marg.The Congress had marched from Rajaji Marg, demanding accountability over the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak, action over the alleged police crackdown on student protesters, and the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Several party leaders and workers were detained as police dispersed the demonstrators.The protest came a day after clashes between Delhi Police and demonstrators during a march to Parliament over the paper leak, an incident that has sparked a political row and led to repeated disruptions in both Houses of Parliament.The Monsoon Session of Parliament began on July 20 and is scheduled to conclude on August 13.
Article Summary: Congress MP Jairam Ramesh's remarks came after Delhi Police detained opposition leaders. He added that Union home minister Amit Shah, under whose ministry the Delhi Police functions, must make a statement on the police action against protesters. The Monsoon Session of Parliament began on July 20 and is scheduled to conclude on August 13.

Article Title: Kalyan Banerjee exits TMC's Martyr's day rally after reported spat with Mamata
Article Text: NEW DELHI: Trinamool Congress MP Kalyan Banerjee on Tuesday reportedly left the party's Martyrs' Day rally in Kolkata after a verbal exchange with party supremo Mamata Banerjee According to a report by news agency PTI, Mamata was upset with Kalyan over exceeding his allotted speaking time at the rally, where he spoke for around 23 minutes.Party sources told PTI that the list of speakers at the rally near the Birla Planetarium was unusually long, and all leaders had been instructed to keep their speeches brief so that everyone could be accommodated.Kalyan Banerjee, however, continued speaking for nearly 23 minutes, launching a blistering attack on the BJP as well as the breakaway Trinamool faction led by Leader of Opposition Ritabrata Banerjee."Mamata Banerjee alone is the Trinamool. The BJP-backed stage has been set up there, but the workers are not with them. Those BJP-sponsored MLAs dreaming of taking over are only digging their own grave," he said.Sources present on the dais claimed that Mamata, who arrived on the stage while Kalyan Banerjee was addressing the gathering, gestured to him to wrap up his speech. When he continued, senior party leader Kunal Ghosh also intervened, they said.After the speech ended, Mamata Banerjee allegedly expressed her displeasure over the delay, leading to a brief exchange of words before the MP walked off the stage and headed straight to his Kalighat residence without waiting for the speeches of party national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee or the TMC chief.Sources in the Mamata camp said the delay forced the organisers to drop several scheduled speakers from the programme, including Lok Sabha MPs Mahua Moitra and Kirti Azad, Rajya Sabha MPs Dola Sen and Sagarika Ghose, and Rajeev Kumar.Kalyan Banerjee, however, rejected reports of any spat with Mamata Banerjee."I left after my speech as I had other engagements. There was no altercation. Had there been one, would I have gone to the front of the stage and addressed the gathering the way I did?" he said.The Trinamool Congress did not issue any official statement on the reported episode.The development assumes political significance as Kalyan Banerjee is among the few senior leaders who have remained steadfastly with Mamata Banerjee after the Trinamool split following the BJP's victory in the 2026 West Bengal Assembly elections.Recently, he publicly criticised Abhishek Banerjee during a dispute over legal representation in a Calcutta High Court case, saying Mamata Banerjee would have to decide "who would remain in the party -- Abhishek or me".The differences were subsequently resolved, with both leaders publicly expressing mutual respect.
Article Summary: Trinamool Congress MP Kalyan Banerjee reportedly left the party's Martyrs' Day rally in Kolkata after a verbal exchange with party supremo MamataBanerjee. Mamata was upset with him over exceeding his allotted speaking time at the rally, where he spoke for around 23 minutes. Sources present on the dais claimed that Mamata, who arrived on the stage while he was addressing the gathering, gestured to him to wrap up.

Article Title: 'TVK covering up misrule, public will teach befitting lesson': Stalin slams CM Vijay over DMK MLA's arrest
Article Text:
Tamil Nadu CM Vijay; DMK supremo MK Stalin

(With PTI inputs)

NEW DELHI: DMK supremo MK Stalin on Tuesday hit out at Tamil Nadu chief minister C Joseph Vijay over the arrest of a party MLA, warning that the people would teach the ruling TVK a "befitting lesson" if it tried to "cover up its misrule" through arrests."If this government thinks it can cover up its misrule with arrests, the people themselves will teach a fitting lesson to such arrogance," Stalin, Vijay's predecessor as chief minister, said in an X post.He referred to an editorial in an English daily that criticised the TVK-led Tamil Nadu government for initiating criminal proceedings over political criticism.Stalin's remarks came amid an escalating confrontation between the ruling TVK and the principal opposition DMK. On Monday, DMK MLA GV Markandeyan was arrested over his purported "we will crush your bones" remark allegedly directed at chief minister Vijay.After several hours of questioning, Markandeyan was produced before a local court, which remanded him to judicial custody.Stalin condemned the police action, describing it as an attempt to criminalise "political criticism", and warned of consequences.TVK, launched by Vijay in February 2024, emerged as the single-largest party in the Tamil Nadu assembly elections in April and later formed the government with the support of the Congress, VCK, IUML, and the Left parties.Vijay, an actor-turned-politician, succeeded Stalin as chief minister after the DMK government was voted out following a single term in office.
Article Summary: Stalin's remarks come amid an escalating confrontation between the ruling TVK and the principal opposition DMK. On Monday, DMK MLA GV Markandeyan was arrested over his purported "we will crush your bones" remark allegedly directed at chief minister Vijay.

Article Title: 'Rahul wanted a confrontation': Jitendra Singh details fallout of talks with Congress leader
Article Text:
Jitendra Singh details fallout of talks with Congress leader

NEW DELHI: Union minister Jitendra Singh accused the opposition leader Rahul Gandhi of shifting his demands during talks over the Congress protest at Lok Kalyan Marg outside Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence, alleging that the Congress leader was more interested in "confrontation" than resolving the issue through dialogue.Speaking to news agency ANI, Singh said he and Union home secretary Govind Mohan were deputed by the government to meet senior Congress leaders after they began a sit-in protest at a location that, according to him, was not designated for demonstrations and was causing inconvenience to the public."It was learned that Congress Party National President Mallikarjun Kharge and other senior leaders like Rahul Gandhi, Priyanka Gandhi and other had suddenly started a protest. It was also reported that the place was not very conducive to a sit-in protest. It was causing significant inconvenience to the public," he said.The minister said the government sent the two officials to persuade the Congress leaders to shift the discussion to Parliament instead of continuing the protest.According to Singh, Rahul told them he would immediately end the sit-in if the government agreed to hold a discussion in Parliament on the NEET issue and related education matters."Discussions were held with the top leadership of the government, and we were told to assure them on behalf of the government that the government has no objection.
