The government is ready to discuss NEET in Parliament, both related issues and the ongoing protests," Singh said.However, Singh alleged that Rahul then introduced a new condition, demanding the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan.The Union minister said he told the Congress leader that "changing the goalpost" was unfair, but Gandhi maintained that it was his prerogative.Singh further alleged that despite the government's willingness to discuss the issue, Rahul Gandhi insisted on continuing the protest. He further alleged that despite the government's willingness to discuss the issue, Rahul Gandhi insisted on continuing the protest."It's very unfortunate that the LoP behaves this way, changing his stance so quickly. This means he's in a mood of conflict. He doesn't want to cooperate with the government to discuss or advance public interest issues. They've come with the intention of confrontation, conflict, and finding an issue," Singh said.Congress party launched a protest outside PM's residence demanding education minister's resignation over the NEET paper leak case. This comes a day after massive 'Sansad Chalo' march by the Cockroach Janta Party. Rahul also called for people to join the protest."An attack on students is an attack on every Indian family. Prime Minister Modi thinks he can escape without giving answers, without facing any consequences - he won't be able to, not at all this time. I appeal to every patriotic Indian who believes that our students should get justice - join our dharna in front of the Prime Minister's residence," he said."The voice of India's students cannot be ignored. An attack on students is an attack on every Indian family. PM Modi believes he can get away without answers, without consequences. He cannot. Not this time. I ask every patriotic Indian who believes our students deserve justice - join us in dharna in front of the Prime Minister’s residence. The voice of India’s students will not be ignored," he added.Meanwhile, the CJP protest continued at the Jantar Mantar with top political leaders like Sharad Pawar, Arvind Kejriwal, Atishi, Supriya Sule joining the protest.
Article Summary: Congress party launched a protest outside PM's residence demanding education minister's resignation over the NEET paper leak case. This comes a day after massive 'Sansad Chalo' march by the Cockroach Janta Party. Rahul also called for people to join the protest.

Article Title: Evening news wrap: Sonam Wangchuk moved to private hospital, Congress protests at PM's house & more
Article Text:
.

Sonam Wangchuk transferred to Medanta Hospital after Delhi HC order; Centre has no objection but insists on medical supervision.

transferred to Medanta Hospital after Delhi HC order; Centre has no objection but insists on medical supervision. Four Indian crew members killed in Russian missile attack on cargo ship off Odesa; Moscow says in touch with New Delhi.

Rahul and Priyanka Gandhi lead Congress protest outside PM Modi's residence, Union minister Jitendra Singh meets them urges them to stop.

E20 petrol may reduce fuel efficiency by 3-5% in older vehicles but does not damage engines, government informs Parliament; no plan to revert to E0 or E10.

Enzo Fernandez says wearing Argentina shirt is "greatest honor" after red card in World Cup final loss to Spain.

Here are the top 5 stories of the day

Delhi high court allows Sonam Wangchuk's shift to private hospital as fast enters fourth week

Russia says 'in touch' with India after missile strike kills 4 Indian sailors off Ukraine

Gandhi siblings lead Congress protest outside PM's residence, demand Modi's ouster

E20 petrol may hit older vehicles' mileage by up to 5%, but engines safe, govt tells Parliament

Argentina star breaks silence after red card in World Cup final heartbreak

The Delhi High Court on Tuesday ordered the immediate transfer of activist Sonam Wangchuk from Safdarjung Hospital to Medanta Hospital in Gurugram, amid his ongoing hunger strike over the NEET paper leak. The court directed Wangchuk to abide by medical advice. The Centre said it had "no objection" to the transfer but urged he remain under continuous medical supervision. Wangchuk's wife had challenged a single judge's order refusing his transfer. Read full story. Russia said it is "in touch" with its partners in New Delhi after four Indians were killed aboard the MV Golden Leo off Ukraine's Odesa coast, following a Russian missile strike. India summoned the Russian chargé d'affaires over the incident and condemned the attack, saying targeting commercial shipping and civilian crew members was "deplorable." One of the victims was identified as 26-year-old merchant navy officer Akhil Joyan from Kerala. Read full story. Congress leaders Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra led a protest outside Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence on Tuesday, demanding his resignation over allegations of corruption and failure on issues including NEET paper leaks and the Ram Temple donation controversy.Party workers raised slogans as the Congress stepped up its attack on the government. Follow top developments. The government informed Parliament that E20 petrol may reduce fuel efficiency by around 3 to 5 per cent in some older vehicles originally designed for E10 fuel, but there is no evidence of widespread engine damage. Minister of State for Petroleum Suresh Gopi said fuel economy is influenced by several factors. The government said there are no plans to return to E0 or E10 petrol. Read full story.

Argentina midfielder Enzo Fernandez spoke publicly for the first time after his side's 1-0 extra-time defeat to Spain in the World Cup final, saying he remains proud to represent his country despite the painful end to their title defence. Fernandez was sent off late in the contest after receiving two yellow cards. "Wearing my country's shirt is the greatest honor of my career," he said. Read full story.
Article Summary: The Delhi High Court ordered the immediate transfer of activist Sonam Wangchuk from Safdarjung Hospital to Medanta Hospital in Gurugram. Russia said it is "in touch" with its partners in New Delhi after four Indians were killed aboard the MV Golden Leo off Ukraine's Odesa coast. Congress leaders Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra led a protest outside Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence.

Article Title: Rahul, Kharge, Mamata, Akhilesh, Kejriwal: Opposition unites for CJP demands; Centre responds
Article Text:
Opposition unites for CJP demands

Which political parties are protesting

What govt said

NEW DELHI: Several opposition parties came out in support of the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP)'s protest againt paper leak of NEET-UG and other competitive exams and their demand for the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan.The central government responded but the talks did not work out. The development comes a day after the CJP's "Sansad Chalo" march which witnessed police crackdown on Monday.Top Congress leaders Mallikarjun Kharge, Rahul Gandhi, Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, KC Venugopal and several party workers staged a protest outside the prime minister's residence.The party is demanding the resignation of the prime minister and the home minister."We have marched to PM Modi’s house to demand answers from him for the brutalities against young students yesterday. The Government doesn’t want to take any accountability or does it want to have a debate on it in Parliament. PM and HM must resign for destroying the future of India’s youth," Lok Sabha leader of opposition Rahul Gandhi said in a post on X.Aam Aadmi Party chief Arvind Kejriwal and leader Atishi joined the CJP protest at Jantar Mantar.Moreover, NCP(SP) chief Sharad Pawar and Lok Sabha member Supriya Sule also joined the protesters.Trinamool Congress (TMC) chief Mamata Banerjee expressed the willingness to join the protests in Delhi if the need arises while also calling for INDIA bloc unity."We supported CJP from the beginning. If need be, I will visit Delhi and join their protest," ANI quoted her as saying."The time has come to be united under the umbrella of the INDIA Alliance against the BJP. I request all parties of the nation and the entire country, and in Bengal also," she added.Samajwadi Party (SP) MP Dimple Yadav was one of the major political leaders who actively participated in the protest on Monday.Questioning the police action against the protesters, Yadav said: "These children had come all the way to the Parliament building because they wanted to tell the government that your system has fallen ill, it is not working. Those innocent children were beaten with batons, the daughters' clothes were torn."The central government has not responded to the demands of the resignations yet. However, talks were held with the CJP and the opposition to pacify the protests.Union minister Jitendra Singh reached Lok Kalyan Marg and spoke with Rahul Gandhi over Congress's protest.Singh later informed that the Centre had agreed to Rahul's initial demand to hold discussions in the Parliament over NEET irregularities, but the opposition leader later back-tracked from his demand and also demanded for education minister's resignation."Shri Rahul Gandhi put forward the demand that if the government agrees to discuss in Parliament all issues related to NEET and the associated movement, he would immediately end his sit-in. Within a few moments, after taking permission from the government's top leadership, Shri Rahul Gandhi was assured that his demand has been accepted, and the government is ready to discuss in Parliament all issues related to NEET and the associated movement," he said."When this information was conveyed to Shri Rahul Gandhi, he said that he would not agree to this alone but also sought assurance for the resignation of the Union Education Minister. He said that now I have two demands: discussion in Parliament and the resignation of the Union Education Minister. When he was reminded that earlier he had only demanded discussion, he said that now my demands have changed. When an attempt was made to humbly explain to him that for a senior leader like him to backtrack from his word so quickly does not befit him, he said that it is his prerogative. When the Home Secretary tried to explain that this site is not suitable for a sit-in, his response was that it is also his prerogative to sit wherever he wants," he said.Union minister JP Nadda also held talks with the protesters on Monday when the CJP submitted its demands."This morning, for the first time, a proposal came from the protesters to hold talks with the government, and our discussions have been ongoing since 11.50am. The meeting took place in a cordial atmosphere. An initial verbal discussion was held in detail with their delegation, and they submitted a written petition to me around 4pm. I have requested all the protesters to end their sit-in and assist the administration in restoring normalcy," Nadda said.Prime Minister Narendra Modi said the government acted firmly against those behind the NEET paper leak to safeguard students' futures, urging states to work with the Centre to eliminate the menace in the national interest. The remarks came at a meeting of the NDA MPs on Tuesday."At the same time, the prime minister said, to ensure that students' future was not affected, the NEET re-examination was given priority, conducted successfully, and the results were declared without delay," Union minister Kiren Rijiju said after the meeting.Protests continued at Jantar Mantar as hundreds of students and their supporters returned to Jantar Mantar a day after their march to Parliament, saying the alleged use of force by police had only reinforced their determination to press on with the agitation.The protest site remained crowded through the afternoon, with students, parents and supporters raising slogans over the alleged irregularities in the NEET-UG examination and reiterating their demand for education minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation.
Article Summary: Opposition parties come out in support of Cockroach Janta Party (CJP)'s protest againt paper leak of NEET-UG and other competitive exams. The development comes a day after the CJP's "Sansad Chalo" march which witnessed police crackdown on Monday.

Article Title: 'Article 370 means nothing without statehood': Omar Abdullah's reply to Mufti over J&K special status demand
Article Text:
Srinagar: Jammu and Kashmir chief minister Omar Abdullah addresses a press conference, in Srinagar. (PTI photo)

(With PTI inputs)

NEW DELHI: Jammu and Kashmir chief minister Omar Abdullah on Tuesday said that statehood was the "foundation" for restoring the Union territory's special status.The opposition Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) had declined an invitation from Abdullah's National Conference (NC) to join a protest in Delhi, saying the demonstration should have focused on the restoration of Article 370 , which granted special status to Jammu and Kashmir before it was revoked by the Modi government in August 2019."Some people tell us to talk about (restoration of) Article 370 and J-K's special status first... I want to ask them what does Article 370 or 371 mean without statehood?" Abdullah told reporters in Srinagar, without naming PDP president and former chief minister Mehbooba Mufti."Whatever special status J&K would have, be it under Article 370 or anything else, the point is how to share the powers between the Centre and J&K. There is no sharing of powers between the Centre and a UT because a UT does not have any powers. All powers of a UT lie either with the Centre or the Lok Bhavan. All Cabinet decisions go to Lok Bhavan first," the chief minister said.Abdullah described statehood as the foundation for restoring Jammu and Kashmir's special status."Where is the state list without the statehood? It is all Union list. That is why we say that to reach there, we have to lay a foundation, then we can build a building on that," he added.Abdullah further stated that the NC had not forgotten the issue of special status."We passed the resolution (on special status) on the first day of the assembly session after government formation. It (resolution) is still alive. This resolution can be discussed only after we have statehood," he said.He also assured the people of Jammu and Kashmir that the party remained committed to the promises it had made."I assure my people we stand by our promises and will remain steadfast. A new strategy and programme will soon be decided by the party leadership," the chief minister added.
Article Summary: Jammu and Kashmir chief minister Omar Abdullah said that statehood was the "foundation" for restoring the Union territory's special status. The opposition Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) had declined an invitation from Abdullah's National Conference (NC) to join a protest in Delhi.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Aristotle: 'A tyrant must appear deeply religious. People are less likely to rebel because they think God is on his side'
Article Text:
Aristotle

"A tyrant must appear deeply religious. People are less likely to fear injustice from a ruler they believe is pious. They are also less likely to rebel because they think God is on his side. "More than two millennia after his death, Aristotle continues to shape the way we understand politics and power. Among the many ideas attributed to the ancient Greek philosopher, one observation from his seminal work Politics has remained particularly enduring for its stark assessment of how tyrants sustain their rule.The quote is a modern paraphrase rather than Aristotle's exact words, it accurately captures an argument he made in Politics more than 2,300 years ago.Born in 384 BC in the Greek city of Stagira, Aristotle is regarded as one of the greatest philosophers in history. A student of Plato and later the teacher of Alexander the Great, he made lasting contributions to philosophy, politics, ethics, logic, biology and natural science. Many of his ideas continue to influence modern political theory, with Politics remaining one of the foundational works on the study of governments, constitutions and the exercise of power.Written in the fourth century BC, Politics is Aristotle's comprehensive study of how states are organised and governed. Rather than proposing a single ideal system, the work examines different forms of government, including monarchy, aristocracy, democracy, oligarchy and tyranny.Book V, from which the famous quote is derived, focuses on political instability, revolutions and the methods rulers use to preserve their authority. The quote is a modern paraphrase of a passage found in Politics, Book V, Chapter 11."He should appear to be particularly earnest in the service of the Gods; for if men think that a ruler is religious and has a reverence for the Gods, they are less afraid of suffering injustice at his hands, and they are less disposed to conspire against him, because they believe him to have the very Gods fighting on his side."According to Aristotle, if subjects believe their ruler is deeply religious and respectful of divine law, they are less likely to suspect him of acting unjustly. They may also hesitate to conspire against him because they believe the ruler enjoys the favour and protection of the gods.In other words, the appearance of piety can become a source of political authority, making obedience seem both morally and divinely justified.The philosopher's point was that people often judge rulers not only by their actions but also by the image they project. A ruler who appears virtuous, just and devout is more likely to inspire trust and loyalty than one who openly relies on coercion.Importantly, Aristotle was describing how tyrants historically maintained power, not prescribing how rulers ought to behave.Aristotle's observation endures because it is less about religion and more about the psychology of power. His insight reminds us that throughout history, public perception has often been as important to political authority as military or legal strength.
Article Summary: The quote is a modern paraphrase rather than Aristotle's exact words. It accurately captures an argument he made in Politics more than 2,300 years ago. A ruler who appears virtuous, just and devout is more likely to inspire trust and loyalty than one who openly relies on coercion.

Article Title: 'If someone dies .... ': Delhi Police probes alleged chats ahead of CJP's 'Sansad Chalo' march
Article Text:
Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) supporters during a protest march, at Sansad Marg, in New Delhi (Picture credit: PTI)

Chats allegedly discussed communication during internet shutdown

Police examining messages on 'sacrifice' and mobilisation

Multiple FIRs registered after violence

NEW DELHI: Delhi Police is examining alleged chat messages exchanged among participants of the Cockroach Janta Party's (CJP) 'Sansad Chalo' march that turned violent on Monday, with some conversations suggesting preparations to maintain communication during a possible internet shutdown and others referring to sacrificing lives for the cause.Police sources cited by news agency PTI said on Tuesday that investigators were verifying the authenticity of the chats and the identities of those involved before drawing any conclusions.The alleged messages are being analysed along with CCTV footage, drone visuals, mobile phone videos and other electronic evidence collected during the investigation into the clashes, which left 118 police personnel and around 70 protesters injured, besides damaging 15-20 government vehicles.According to PTI, some of the alleged messages discussed alternative ways to stay connected if internet services were disrupted during the protest."After reaching the protest site you might not get network in your device. Police last time also used network jammers and this time too can do so. However, it is very important to stay connected to the group you came with," one of the alleged messages read.Investigators said participants had also shared links to applications that could reportedly function using Bluetooth signals in the absence of internet connectivity."Make sure all of your group have the same app installed and are already connected before entering the protest site," another alleged message stated.Police said they were examining the applications mentioned in the chats and would take action based on the findings.The alleged conversations also contained politically charged remarks and mobilisation messages.One participant claimed the following day would be the "last day of Modi Government."Some messages also referred to the possibility of participants sacrificing their lives during the protest."Dekho yarr jo jaan dene tak ko taiyaar woh ye sunlo ke kam se kam tumhara ehsan to rahega aane wali pidiyon par, aane wale bacho par (if someone died, future generations would remember such sacrifice)," one alleged message read.Another message stated, "Bhai agar kisi ki maut hoti hai to usme to sarkar pe zyada pressure aayega (if someone dies, then it will create a lot of pressure on the government)."Police said investigators were examining the context in which these remarks were made and were trying to determine whether they reflected personal opinions exchanged among participants or were part of any coordinated effort linked to the violence.Forensic examination of the digital communications is underway, with cyber experts assisting in establishing the origin, circulation and authenticity of the messages."Every message was being subjected to technical verification before being treated as evidence," a police source said, adding that the investigation remained evidence-based and further legal action would depend on corroborative material.The alleged chats surfaced as Delhi Police expanded its probe into the violence during the 'Sansad Chalo' march.Police have so far registered four FIRs and are preparing two more in connection with the violence, vandalism, alleged conspiracy and other incidents linked to the protest.The cases, registered at Parliament Street, Connaught Place and other police stations, invoke provisions of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), the Arms Act and the Prevention of Damage to Public Property Act.Investigators are also examining CCTV footage, drone recordings, mobile phone videos, social media posts and other digital evidence to identify those involved in the clashes.Following the violence, security has been tightened across the national capital with additional police and paramilitary personnel deployed at sensitive locations, government installations and major intersections.
Article Summary: Delhi Police is examining alleged chat messages exchanged among participants of the Cockroach Janta Party's 'Sansad Chalo' march that turned violent on Monday. Some conversations suggest preparations to maintain communication during a possible internet shutdown. Others refer to sacrificing lives for the cause.

Article Title: Stopped at Shambhu border on way to Delhi: Why are farmers protesting again
Article Text:
March to Delhi against India-US trade deal: Farmers depart from Gurudwara Fatehgarh Sahib; to stage sit-in at 'Kisan Ghat'

Why are farmers protesting

NEW DELHI: Farmers from Punjab heading to Delhi for a fresh protest were stopped at the Shambhu border between Punjab and Haryana , where authorities put up barricades and deployed cement blocks to prevent them from proceeding towards the national capital.The proposed four-hour "Kisan Mahapanchayat" was scheduled to be held at Kisan Ghat, the memorial of former Prime Minister Charan Singh, who was also a prominent farmers' leader.Farmers in Punjab have been protesting against the proposed India-United States trade deal, an interim agreement for which was announced after a phone call between Prime Minister Narendra Modi and US President Donald Trump in February.They are demanding the cancellation of the trade deal.The farmers' primary concern is the inclusion of the agriculture and dairy sectors in the proposed agreement. They fear that unrestricted imports of cheaper US products such as maize, soybeans, cotton and dairy items could lead to a fall in domestic crop prices, causing financial losses, particularly for farmers in Punjab and Haryana.According to farmer leader Sarwan Singh Pandher, who was leading the protesters, they are also concerned about the possible entry of genetically modified (GM) crops and agricultural products into India.They reiterated their long-standing demand for a legal guarantee on Minimum Support Price (MSP).Referring to Union minister Piyush Goyal 's statements on the proposed agreement, Pandher told ANI, "Piyush Goyal says there will be no compromise on the dairy and agriculture sectors, but it seems a deal has already been made.""The White House press release mentions cotton, soybean, corn, dry fruits, apples, pears, and other agricultural products under a 'Wide Range Trade Agreement.' 'Wide range' covers everything--it is a comprehensive trade agreement. So, I believe Piyush Goyal is not being truthful with the country. The India-US trade deal seems certain," he alleged.Goyal has been leading negotiations on behalf of India.
Article Summary: Farmers from Punjab heading to Delhi for a fresh protest were stopped at the Shambhu border between Punjab and Haryana. Authorities put up barricades and deployed cement blocks to prevent them from proceeding towards the national capital. The proposed four-hour "Kisan Mahapanchayat" was scheduled to be held at Kisan Ghat.

Article Title: 'Abhishek Banerjee refused to step aside': Madan Mitra quits Mamata's camp, joins Ritabrata Banerjee's TMC faction
Article Text:
Madan Mitra, Ritabrata Banerjee, Mamata Banerjee

Poll Vote & Share your view Do you believe the TMC will recover from the recent defections? Yes, they will regain strength No, it's too late for recovery 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: Trinamool Congress (TMC) MLA Madan Mitra on Wednesday resigned from the Mamata Banerjee-led faction of the party and joined the rebel TMC faction headed by Ritabrata Banerjee, dealing another setback to the former chief minister's camp.His exit comes amid a series of defections from the Mamata Banerjee-led faction, with several senior leaders and MLAs joining the rival group led by Ritabrata Banerjee in recent weeks following the political split in the party.Speaking to reporters after joining the rebel camp, Mitra accused TMC national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee of refusing to step aside despite the party's crisis."I had suggested to Abhishek Banerjee that he step aside for six months or a year. I told him, let us build the party, and then you can come back and take your seat, but he refused. He said, 'I won't leave the party.' The party is sinking; the boat has gone under. People are dying. Yet, the party decided, or rather it was forced to accept, that everyone else could die, but Abhishek had to be saved. It is deeply saddening."Mitra alleged that the party had become centred around one individual instead of functioning collectively."The party belongs to everyone, yet it seems to have been reduced to serving only Abhishek. I earnestly request Mamata ji to come. Let us view this as a marathon. We will surely cross paths along the way. Let us see which horse surges ahead."He said he had resigned from all organisational positions in the Trinamool Congress and no longer considered himself part of the party."I have resigned from all the positions. Though I remain an MLA. I have left everything associated with Trinamool. That means I am no longer a Trinamool MLA in the functional sense.""I have only changed my room, not my house. I am very much in the TMC," Mitra told reporters after meeting the rebel leader.The development comes a day after the Enforcement Directorate (ED) summoned Mitra's wife and his two sons for questioning in connection with its money laundering investigation into the alleged municipal recruitment scam in West Bengal.According to the ED, the summons were issued after investigators found financial transactions involving the family members during the course of the probe. The agency has asked them to appear next week to record their statements.The case relates to alleged irregularities in appointments to various municipal bodies in West Bengal. The ED is investigating the matter under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) based on predicate offences registered by the Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI).The agency had earlier conducted searches at multiple premises linked to Mitra in June this year, while the CBI had also searched his residence in October 2025 as part of its investigation into the same case.
Article Summary: Madan Mitra has joined the rebel TMC faction headed by Ritabrata Banerjee. His exit comes amid a series of defections from the former chief minister's camp. The Enforcement Directorate has summoned Mitra's wife and his two sons for questioning in connection with its money laundering investigation.

Article Title: FIFA president Gianni Infantino asked to resign after being accused of 'destroying the football industry'
Article Text:
FIFA President Gianni Infantino (AP Photo/Jacquelyn Martin)

Fans' group joins criticism

La Liga president Javier Tebas has once again criticised FIFA president Gianni Infantino, saying he should step down over decisions that he believes are harming football.Tebas accused FIFA of putting commercial interests ahead of the sport by expanding international tournaments and increasing fixture congestion.His latest comments come amid discussions about expanding the 2030 World Cup from 48 to 64 teams.Tebas said expanding the World Cup further would damage domestic competitions, which he believes are the backbone of football.“Increasing the number of national teams makes no sense. The football industry isn't just the World Cup, which is the most important event. But not everything can revolve around the World Cup,” he told Italian outlet La Gazzetta dello Sport, as quoted by news agency AP.He also called for Infantino to leave office, saying, “In my opinion, yes, I think his time is up.”Tebas added that many people privately oppose Infantino but fail to act, claiming: “He shouldn't stay, but he won't leave... those who stay silent are perfectly aware of the damage football is suffering.”Tebas also criticised FIFA's hydration breaks and the extended 27-minute halftime during the World Cup final, saying: “Hydration breaks are a lie...It was a lie — and just a break for advertising.”Football Supporters Europe (FSE) also condemned FIFA's handling of the World Cup, calling for major governance reforms.The group accused FIFA of prioritising commercial interests over supporters, pointing to high ticket prices, expensive travel, poor communication and changes to football traditions.FSE argued that the tournament damaged the reputation of both FIFA and the World Cup, concluding that “FIFA is unfit to govern global football” and accusing Infantino of putting financial and political interests ahead of the game's future.
Article Summary: Javier Tebas has called for FIFA president Gianni Infantino to step down. Tebas said expanding the World Cup further would damage domestic competitions. Football Supporters Europe (FSE) also condemned FIFA's handling of the 2014 World Cup. FSE accused FIFA of prioritising commercial interests over supporters.

Article Title: 18-year-old footballer Nolan Wells' family attorney reveals latest on long-awaited autopsy report
Article Text:
Nolan Wells. Image via: GoFundMe

Nolan Wells' family attorney reveals key update on expected autopsy report release

Civil rights leaders demand justice for Nolan Wells

Attorney Ben Crump speaks alongside Elmore Wonsley and Christine Wonsley and Rev. Al Sharpton. Image via: Gerald Herbert/ AP Photo

Why did Nolan Wells' death raise suspicion?

Nolan Wells' family attorney says an independent autopsy report on the 18-year-old's death will be released Wednesday, offering the first outside medical findings since the Mississippi teenager was found dead off Horn Island in early July. Civil rights attorney Ben Crump plans to share preliminary results during a news conference at the NAACP National Convention in Chicago.Wells' parents are expected to attend. The teenager disappeared after a Fourth of July boat trip to the island with friends and was found dead two days later, a case that has drawn national attention over questions of race and the circumstances of his death.Crump has vowed to conduct his own investigation into Wells' death, which followed the July 4 trip to Horn Island, off the Mississippi coast. Wells never returned with his friends, and his body was recovered two days later. Former NFL quarterback Colin Kaepernick paid for the independent autopsy, which Crump said would determine whether Wells had injuries beyond drowning.The Mississippi State Medical Examiner's Office has conducted its own autopsy but hasn't released results, with officials citing a pending toxicology report. Crump has voiced frustration with the pace of the investigation."It's always troublesome to all of us when these investigations just take forever," Crump said.Family and friends gathered at Center Pointe Church in Ocean Springs for Wells' funeral, joined by national civil rights leaders. Wells' mother, Christine Wells-Wonsley, addressed mourners at the service. "Share his stories, and never stop saying his name," she said.The Rev. Al Sharpton officiated the funeral and has pressed local authorities not to rule out foul play before completing their investigation. "We can't have someone in law enforcement saying there was no foul play before there was an investigation," Sharpton said.Filmmaker Tyler Perry covered funeral costs, and reported reward offers for information in the case have ranged from $100,000 to $125,000, attributed in different reports to combinations of Sharpton, Perry, Kaepernick and former NFL player Terrell Owens.Wells was the only Black member of a group of high school friends, all white, who traveled together to Horn Island for the holiday. He was also the only one who didn't return, and questions have persisted over why friends left the island without him, reportedly took his phone and keys, and didn't immediately report him missing.Jackson County Sheriff John Ledbetter has said initial interviews indicated Wells chose to stay behind, believing he would catch a ride from someone else, and that the friends have cooperated with the investigation.Sharpton criticized authorities for suggesting no foul play was suspected before an investigation had concluded. The FBI has confirmed it is assisting local law enforcement, including looking into interstate threats made against people connected to the case. Wells' mother has said she tracked her son's phone through a location-sharing app and has raised concerns that messages may have been deleted before it was recovered.Crump's independent autopsy findings, due Wednesday in Chicago, will mark the first outside medical assessment of Wells' death since his body was recovered. Jackson County's district attorney has said investigation results will go before a grand jury, and Crump's team is expected to review data from Wells' phone alongside law enforcement in the weeks ahead.
Article Summary: Nolan Wells' family attorney says an independent autopsy report on the 18-year- old's death will be released Wednesday. Civil rights attorney Ben Crump plans to share preliminary results during a news conference at the NAACP National Convention in Chicago. The teenager disappeared after a Fourth of July boat trip to the island with friends and was found dead two days later.

Article Title: 'July 20th was trailer, we are ready to be arrested under UAPA and NSA': CJP spokesperson Ashutosh Ranka as Jantar Mantar protest enters day 3
Article Text:
Rejecting claims that the protesters were not "peaceful", Ranka maintained that the July 20 march was peaceful and alleged that the situation escalated only after police used force against students.

NEW DELHI: Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) national spokesperson Ashutosh Ranka on Wednesday warned the Union government that the country's youth would "take them down very badly" if it failed to address the protesters' demands, describing the July 20 demonstration as only a "trailer".Speaking to ANI, Ranka termed the ongoing agitation the "biggest movement in India's post-independence history" and said millions more people could come to Delhi if the government did not accept their demands."This is the biggest movement in India's post-independence history... We have said this very clearly: what happened on 20th was a trailer. If the Govt doesn't agree, millions more will come to Delhi because this time we will not hold back and stay silent... The Govt really needs to get its act together; otherwise, the youth of this country is going to take them down very badly," Ranka said.Rejecting claims that the protesters were not "peaceful", Ranka maintained that the July 20 march was peaceful and alleged that the situation escalated only after police used force against students."A narrative is trying to be built that we will not be peaceful, but we are. The march on 20th was peaceful too but there was chaos only at the point where Police unleashed brutality and beat up students.The youth is sitting here peacefully for 31 days because we follow the principles of Gandhi and Ambedkar," he said.Ranka also said the protesters would not allow any future FIR to be registered against those participating in the agitation and asserted that the protest would not end without such an assurance."We have placed a very clear demand and Abhijeet Dipke had spoken about it. We will not led any kind of FIR or future FIR be lodged against any protester; the protest won't end without that," he said.He added that the leadership of the movement was not afraid of legal action."As far as our team's leadership is concerned, we are not scared of any FIR, we are ready to be arrested under UAPA and NSA from day 1. Jail is a small price to pay for our fight," Ranka said.Calling for political parties to support the movement, Ranka said the protesters would welcome leaders from any party, including the BJP."Be it any political party, even if they are people from the BJP, they should come together because this is a huge movement and the demand is genuine. We welcome all of them," he said.Meanwhile, opposition MPs are set to stage a protest outside Makar Dwar of Parliament on Wednesday, demanding the resignation of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan.The protest has been tentatively scheduled for 10.30am. Opposition legislators will also raise concerns over alleged irregularities in the NEET UG examination and protest against the police action taken against students on July 20.(With inputs from ANI)
Article Summary: CJP national spokesperson Ashutosh Ranka said the July 20 demonstration was only a "trailer" Ranka also said the protesters would not allow any future FIR to be registered against those participating in the agitation. Opposition MPs are set to stage a protest outside Makar Dwar of Parliament on Wednesday.

Article Title: 'Didn't bring sarees for me': Thane woman strangles husband after hitting him with pressure cooker lid
Article Text:
AI Image Used For Representational Purpose Only

THANE: A domestic dispute over a saree allegedly ended in a brutal murder in Badlapur East, where a 30-year-old woman was arrested for allegedly killing her husband and attempting to pass off his death as a heart attack.The accused, identified as Sunita Hariram Singh (30), was arrested on Tuesday after investigators uncovered inconsistencies in her version of events.The victim, Hariram Kishunji Singh (35), died at the couple's rented home on July 19.Police said the couple, natives of Rohtas district in Bihar, had been living in Badlapur for the past nine years with their seven-year-old son.According to the investigation, an argument erupted after Hariram allegedly failed to bring sarees for his wife. The verbal altercation soon turned violent.During the quarrel, the couple's seven-year-old son reportedly ran to the room owner's house seeking help to stop the fight.However, before anyone could intervene, Sunita allegedly assaulted her husband with the lid of a pressure cooker and then strangled him to death, police said.To conceal the crime, she allegedly told the room owner that Hariram had suffered a heart attack after the argument.Based on her statement, police initially registered an Accidental Death Report (ADR).Senior police inspector Nitin Patil of Badlapur East police station said the post-mortem examination raised suspicion after doctors found injuries inconsistent with a natural death."Initially, the woman claimed her husband had died of a heart attack following a quarrel. However, the autopsy confirmed that he had been murdered. We then registered an FIR for murder and, during questioning, the accused confessed to killing her husband," Patil said.Police have booked Sunita under the relevant provisions of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), and further investigation is underway.The seven-year-old boy is being cared for by relatives, police said.
Article Summary: Sunita Hariram Singh, 30, arrested for allegedly killing her husband and attempting to pass off his death as a heart attack. Sunita allegedly assaulted her husband with the lid of a pressure cooker and then strangled him to death. The couple, natives of Rohtas district in Bihar, had been living in Badlapur for the past nine years.

Article Title: 'Not welcome in NYC': Mamdani ramps up attack on 'war criminal' Netanyahu
Article Text:
The mayor also said his administration had examined whether New York City had the authority to arrest Netanyahu if he visited the city.

New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has stepped up his criticism of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu , declaring that the Israeli leader is "not welcome in New York City" and urging the US federal government to enforce the International Criminal Court's (ICC) arrest warrant against him.In a video posted on X on Tuesday, Mamdani accused Netanyahu of committing grave crimes during Israel's military campaign in Gaza, calling him "a war criminal, the architect of a horrific genocide against the Palestinian people."He said Netanyahu was responsible "for the maiming of tens of thousands of children as those who survive undergo amputation without anesthesia. For the targeting of neonatal hospitals and maternity care centers, denying newborns even the chance to live. For the countless people he starved as he blocked food and humanitarian aid from reaching them."Referring to the ICC warrant issued against Netanyahu, Mamdani said, "There is a reason the International Criminal Court issued a warrant for his arrest." He added, "As I've said, I agree with the ICC that Benjamin Netanyahu should be arrested and tried for his crimes as I do for anyone else charged by the ICC."The mayor also said his administration had examined whether New York City had the authority to arrest Netanyahu if he visited the city."My administration has reviewed every avenue available under applicable law to determine whether New York City could execute the International Criminal Court's arrest warrant if Benjamin Netanyahu came here. It is clear that we do not have the independent legal authority to enforce this warrant. The federal government, however, does. And I call on them to join the ICC and execute this warrant," he said.Mamdani ended his remarks by saying, "Benjamin Netanyahu is not welcome in New York City, nor is any other war criminal at large."His latest comments come days after he told The New York Times that his administration was exploring the legal options available under existing law to act on the ICC warrant. The court issued arrest warrants for Netanyahu and other Israeli leaders in 2024 over alleged war crimes and crimes against humanity during and after the October 7, 2023, Hamas attacks. Israel has denied the allegations, while the United States is not a party to the Rome Statute that established the ICC.US President Donald Trump rejected Mamdani's stance, writing on Truth Social that Netanyahu "will not be arrested, in any way, shape, or form" while in the United States. Israel's ambassador to the UN, Danny Danon, also criticised the mayor, accusing him of promoting "Hamas' propaganda". Legal experts have also argued that a New York City mayor has no independent authority to execute an ICC arrest warrant, with such matters falling under the federal government's jurisdiction.
Article Summary: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani has stepped up his criticism of Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. He accused Netanyahu of committing grave crimes during Israel's military campaign in Gaza. The mayor also said his administration had examined whether New York City had the authority to arrest Netanyahu if he visited the city.

Article Title: War cost US over $37 billion, Hegseth says, as strikes on Iran continue for 11 straight days
Article Text: US defence secretary Pete Hegseth said the war against Iran has cost $37.5 billion so far as he defended President Donald Trump's funding request during a heated Senate hearing. Hegseth faced protests and sharp questioning ahead of a proposed $95 billion Republican budget package, which includes military funding alongside aid for farmers and voting law changes backed by the White House.Appearing before the Senate Appropriations Committee, Hegseth urged lawmakers to back the administration's emergency defence spending request, saying the additional funding was essential to sustain military operations."The supplemental war funding is an 'urgent, necessary' injection of funds," Hegseth said, adding that Trump's proposed $1.5 trillion defence budget represented "a generational investment in the military.""Without these funds, we face critical shortfalls," Hegseth testified.The defence secretary was making the case for President Trump's $87.6 billion supplemental funding request, most of which is intended to replenish Pentagon resources following the US military campaign against Iran.Republicans are also preparing a broader $95 billion package covering defence spending, aid for farmers and other White House priorities.Hegseth's testimony was interrupted several times by protesters. The hearing came days after the Pentagon announced that three more American service members had died, taking the US military death toll in the conflict to 17, while more than 100 personnel have been injured since early July.Democrats questioned both the scale of the funding request and the administration's broader strategy. Acccording yo AFP, senate Patty Murray, the committee's top Democrat, warned that the United States risked entering 'another forever war.'"This war is now spiraling out of control again," Murray said.She added, "Trump has told us over 40 times now that a deal is near, and the war will be over soon. But now, he's asking for USD 70 billion more — and for us to just trust him it'll all work out fine."Gen. Dan Caine, Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, and Agriculture Secretary Brooke Rollins also appeared before the committee.Ahead of the hearing, the White House released a statement urging lawmakers to approve the budget resolution "without modification—immediately."The proposed Republican package allocates the bulk of its funding to the military campaign. It also includes $10 billion in assistance for farmers affected by Trump's tariffs and $10 billion for voting law changes expected to include stricter voter identification requirements.The funding package comes as public opinion remains divided over the conflict. According to an AP-NORC poll, most Americans continue to disapprove of Trump's handling of the Iran war. The conflict has also coincided with rising fuel prices, pressure on US military stockpiles and growing concerns over a prolonged military engagement despite Trump's earlier campaign pledge to avoid such wars.Trump also urged House Republicans through a social media post earlier this week to support the budget resolution. Beyond the war funding request, Hegseth also faced scrutiny over his leadership of the Pentagon.Lawmakers further pressed Hegseth to release the Pentagon's report into a deadly strike on an Iranian primary school early in the conflict, warning that they could withhold funding for his official travel if the report is not made public.
Article Summary: US defence secretary Pete Hegseth said the war against Iran has cost $37.5 billion so far. The hearing came days after the Pentagon announced that three more American service members had died, taking the US military death toll in the conflict to 17. Republicans are also preparing a broader $95 billion package covering defence spending and aid for farmers.

Article Title: Govt school bombed in northwest Pakistan; no casualties reported
Article Text:
Representative image

PESHAWAR: Unknown militants blew up a government school in Pakistan's restive Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province, officials said on Tuesday.No casualties were reported in the incident which took place late on Monday, and no group has claimed responsibility for it yet.According to initial reports, unidentified militants planted explosive material at Government Middle School Loi Kharki in the Bajaur district, causing a powerful explosion that extensively damaged the school building.Soon after the incident, law enforcement agencies and other relevant authorities reached the site and launched an investigation.Officials said efforts were underway to determine the nature of the explosion, identify those responsible and ascertain the motive behind the attack.Further investigation is in progress, officials added.According to investigative briefs from Human Rights Watch and the Human Rights Commission of Pakistan, militants target educational structures for specific operational reasons.A vast majority of the targeted attacks directly hit girls' institutions to enforce extremist ideological bans against female literacy.
Article Summary: Unknown militants blew up a government school in Pakistan's restive Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province. No casualties were reported in the incident which took place late on Monday, and no group has claimed responsibility for it yet. Law enforcement agencies and other relevant authorities reached the site and launched an investigation.
